name: With Honor Action
subject: Afghan Allies: Honoring Service and Keeping Our Word
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/1d96ccec9eec/bats-brews-and-a-birthday-salute-to-the-us-army-14053661?e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | |
---
| | | | [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=9f49605ff0&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| Friends,

Earlier this week, Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-IA) and Rep. Jason Crow (D-CO), both members of the [For Country Caucus](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=c51a766bc3&e=33f53a5b1a), introduced the bipartisan [Afghan Adjustment Act](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=27fb6f9c82&e=33f53a5b1a).

This bill works to give Afghans who came to the U.S. under humanitarian parole a clear path to permanent legal status, strengthens the Special Immigrant Visa program for Afghan translators and interpreters, and coordinates federal relocation efforts. With Honor Action applauds this bipartisan effort, led by veterans and allies in Congress, as a vital step in honoring America’s promises.
---
| [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=3c75169540&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
_Rep. Miller-Meeks with members of the Afghan Female Tactical Platoon, an elite, all-women unit that served shoulder-to-shoulder with U.S. Special Operations Forces on night raids and high-risk missions to engage with Afghan women and children where male troops could not._
| Since 2021, With Honor Action and the For Country Caucus have fought to keep faith with our Afghan partners, helping pass laws that cut red tape, secure significant funding for evacuations and resettlement efforts, and expand visa opportunities. We’ve joined forces with veterans’ groups nationwide to urge Congress to move quickly, because every delay puts lives at risk.

Veterans understand loyalty — it’s the bond forged in service. This bill is our chance to show the world that America stands by those who stood with us. With Honor Action will keep working until they are signed into law.

🔗 Read more about the bill [here](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=18b832ff86&e=33f53a5b1a).
---
|
---
| [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=e83385c02e&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| America faces growing cyber threats, and today those responsibilities are divided across multiple service branches. With Honor Co-Founder and CEO Rye Barcott will serve on this newly formed bipartisan commission that will examine whether establishing a dedicated U.S. Cyber Force could improve the organization and effectiveness of our military's cyber capabilities.

Rep. Morgan Luttrell (R-TX) and Rep. Chrissy Houlahan (D-PA), both veterans and members of the For Country Caucus, have played key roles in advancing cyber policy in Congress and laying the groundwork for this review.

Read more about the commission here: [New Commission to Examine How to Create Independent Cyber Force](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=82ea95a2de&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
|
---
| | | | Please follow With Honor Action on Twitter via this [link](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=008f4736da&e=33f53a5b1a),
and consider forwarding this to a friend who may be interested in learning -- and doing -- more for our country.
---

body:

---
Peter,
---
The media finally said the quiet part out loud. Straight from _Politico_ : **“Tish James is all in on Mamdani”**
---
Letitia James – the NY Attorney General and the SOLE remaining prosecutor against President Trump – launched a full-court press to elevate Zohran Mamdani and called him the __next Obama__.
---

Fellow Republican, If you think Indiana is safe from the effects of the border crisis, we’ve got news for you...

**The drugs flooding into our communities? ** **_They’re coming from cartels that crossed the border._**
**The strain on public services? ** **_Fueled by waves of illegal immigration._**
**The rising crime? ** **_It’s not just a border town issue anymore._**

That’s why Indiana Republicans aren’t just talking — _**[we’re taking action.](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/U6tsZgPtZKaAJ_vFFRL1dKVOZUY8gArCOg-zUqcEPIB3dgwJTV5K9y0sAhQzFemikF43AfY1AwcthJmTDRECuutkRfoQoOf_qr_5uptkQ3IEsQhzCIUlFpaHXYCeLgpW7_Nr9Ro29M_mZHPTqdS7Y-hEMERz3oW9HhbqsauV9mR7lNjMh8IjBCgqjmBC86wI283sJ3uCBNYWuHNcKxS8VoONjk0OkoVe6FqZQ1_RneMbx8E2sJPFOTl1Ue7p7dR2LabC_7nilLZsPWGW4YKHjxn9wVEsMOiTLRiLXhsGVNbKU0GdsreBc7DHyUY1hHbQ3_kHitqCFg9XvSP8RpK_U9xgFKgd3vDU0-69fJdRGxR7Fqu0V4CLEOiT83fBjQilOWkagAMSoAkQojbpHs4jkfYxNwIdTJQ_ZHLJ4yu7B61LYpyszYH70rMtOzPygopQdRxGxMwunkv3-nPLJgMj5VWmK63lw15QJtp9XyerVeT1viRDo9i9zSAJpYkofg7nNl4zVVrx8xWbFTJqeQJ2MrjV6cfj48fxKGtn5JZQhyqLEXwBQHGqhtRQliXu1ZdG0s5j3FaV-o2RqLIHI2OnIk_k_RSvPDoHEEJ6lvJyqA/B0MGh4l3k5q2AFTO_dqT1c4vfLrcr8SQ/11)**_
Our Republican majority passed HB 1393 to make sure **every sheriff flags and reports illegal immigrants after arrest** and Governor Braun signed EO 25-29, directing state police to **cooperate with ICE, mobilize the Guard, and stop illegal hiring** through E-Verify.

While national Democrats double down on lawlessness, **Indiana Republicans are standing with President Trump and defending our borders. **

But we need your help to keep going.
---

name: Voter Action Project
subject: This is a pivotal moment
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
WaPo - Opinion: A frantic warning from 100 leading experts: Our democracy is in grave danger [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=080825_1&amount=25]
We’ll keep this short, Team: those experts are right.
The attacks of the last few years in our state legislatures across the country targeting the right to vote, especially minority communities, are an assault on our democracy.
Now Trump, Musk, DOGE, and the MAGA movement want to privatize our postal service and harm mail-in voting , which has helped millions of Americans vote -- not just in the pandemic, but going back to the states that adopted the widespread practice in the early 90's.
“ Our entire democracy is now at risk ,” they wrote. “ History will judge what we do at this moment. ”
With so much at stake now -- not just with voting rights -- we've got to push our efforts to fight back into overdrive ASAP.

name: Elise Stefanik
subject: Completing the voter verification process
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Elise, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Elise for Congress and E-PAC
body:
I’m making a personal request...
| **__A Personal Request from the House GOP Leadership Chair, Rep. Elise Stefanik_ _**
---
Peter,
---
President Trump has expanded our Party’s electoral coalition like never before.
---
**We now have far more voters than the Democrats – but that total includes _MILLIONS_ of “less engaged” voters who don’t always turn out when President Trump isn’t on the ballot.**
---
Our path to victory in the midterms is simple: If every “likely” Republican voter shows up to cast a ballot, we’ll win in a landslide.
---
_But if we get complacent and our turnout dips, we’ll _LOSE_ our House Majority to radical, Far Left Democrats!_
---
**Given the new dynamics of our electoral coalition, the GOP’s official 2025 Voter Verification project has never been more critical.**
---
We need to know exactly who we can count on to show up and cast their ballot for Republicans in the midterms.
---
You see, every Republican voter we confirm as “VERIFIED” for the midterms is one less voter we need to spend time and money targeting between now and Election Day.
---
_That way, we can prioritize spending our limited financial resources to target the millions of less engaged “likely” Republican voters and focus on getting them to the polls._
---
**As House GOP Leadership Chair, it’s my duty to ensure our existing GOP voter file is _100% VERIFIED_ before the end of the year. **
---
And when I say “100% verified,” any old response isn’t enough...
---
I need to make sure that __every_ _single__ Republican voter we have on file has submitted a complete, up-to-date Voter Verification survey measuring their voter intensity and confirming their intention to vote for Republicans in the midterms.
---
That’s why I’m reaching out to you today, Peter...
---
[**Please complete our survey to ensure that YOUR voter profile is fully verified and confirm that you’re one of the most engaged Republicans voters who we can count on to show up and vote in the midterms.**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=1f1181bb5d7f4127966f301acc3729bc&_e=9oHXKLvZFRPCB7Jfe0jJHmiWgt1YYWiypPmNhnzoZ8DV3G8abAB-b8jfXcaIPwhmlyozHW4S0FTzoWmHDL_RDAWvH0ASELfpvU9h10PZdyGXbcUjsJyIrEt1-oW2PE6MLc7xMIrfY_sOPlTEqGO2CCsZEUPLlGDErgKn5LckmBqbwP8P-FN33_1U7JMKI9sr-jjuKp6J10rmpYLsuZuDpTkJb3CVZVB53LGM7KdE1g3fODexWa0K998p9TnxaiD55rTiOiMivJymVzj_16HsIbwItsc14bsa52KKv2NZgH0ItD5utcDmuVFcSvm_aDC2qw-QdfyeIXUJ0whrxD7Ophx58Tukg1uVtQw7-n2hxE1mKlajRf_VDF_pTFhYgEkhjkD1lQTr2dxMwF0kg0kx6NT-M6CFnNEB8NnGcc5E2qjrg5tQBNBJ0ABO8xnMj0rmU_O78pigqkmtBSRRVW5xg9wElCSOw9W5_b4SPLmu3-mJFa6RPgQ37W_Vh3pf_mJ4NzNS-0i41R8LAdyPYppTH_BCIgWnjc4nW4IfERS2ZSHvAj7zsxYjYCkJ2OIJBXkhZykMAclG3uwoR9K8PDXYiqoy6U_8YMpq10JcRxS-h6Q%3D)

clean_body: I’m making a personal request... | A Personal Request from the House GOP Leadership Chair, Rep. Elise Stefanik Peter, President Trump has expanded our Party’s electoral coalition like never before. We now have far more voters than the Democrats – but that total includes MILLIONS of “less engaged” voters who don’t always turn out when President Trump isn’t on the ballot. Our path to victory in the midterms is simple: If every “likely” Republican voter shows up to cast a ballot, we’ll win in a landslide. But if we get complacent and our turnout dips, we’ll LOSE our House Majority to radical, Far Left Democrats! Given the new dynamics of our electoral coalition, the GOP’s official 2025 Voter Verification project has never been more critical. We need to know exactly who we can count on to show up and cast their ballot for Republicans in the midterms. You see, every Republican voter we confirm as “VERIFIED” for the midterms is one less voter we need to spend time and money targeting between now and Election Day. That way, we can prioritize spending our limited financial resources to target the millions of less engaged “likely” Republican voters and focus on getting them to the polls. As House GOP Leadership Chair, it’s my duty to ensure our existing GOP voter file is 100% VERIFIED before the end of the year. And when I say “100% verified,” any old response isn’t enough... I need to make sure that every single Republican voter we have on file has submitted a complete, up-to-date Voter Verification survey measuring their voter intensity and confirming their intention to vote for Republicans in the midterms. That’s why I’m reaching out to you today, Peter... Official 2025 GOP Voter Verification Survey Scope: Measuring Voter Intensity Participant: dpwillis67@gmail.com Survey ID #: 175644-866 Submission time remaining: UNKNOWN Response Status: PENDING | Thank you, Rep. body:
What the heck are you waiting for!?
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93343?refcode=em20250808-heartland-patriots-rob-sand&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9237%2E614464%2ENIdLPc)
Peter, if you’ve been following Iowa’s pivotal governor’s race, and you’ve yet to join Rob Sand’s campaign, I’ve got only one question for you:
**What the heck are you waiting for!?**
_I mean c’mon, Rob checks all the boxes: Born and raised in Iowa, a bowhunter, a dedicated public servant and, let’s get real —**Democrats' only shot at winning the Iowa Governor’s race.**_
Rob’s building a grassroots movement that’s growing by the day, and he’s doing that by speaking directly with Iowans in their communities, having the tough conversations.
**I’ve got plenty to say about this race, but before I go on:[Rob’s fundraising has taken a dip recently, so if you’ve been waiting to chip in to his campaign, well there’s no time like the present. Rob’s campaign has some big budget decisions coming up, so chipping in a split grassroots donation would go a long way!](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93343?refcode=em20250808-9237&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9237%2E614464%2ENIdLPc)**
**[Split a Donation!](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93343?refcode=em20250808-9237&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9237%2E614464%2ENIdLPc)**
Peter, it’s no freakin’ secret: Iowa has been trending in the wrong direction.
And if you’re looking for the root cause, then look no further than the national Republican insiders who have been calling the shots for years.
Iowa Republicans are a mess right now, and they’re clambering over one another to try and lock down the backing of the same special interests who want to drag Iowa backwards.
**I’m not just blowing smoke, Peter: This is very much a winnable race, and Rob’s currently in the driver’s seat.**
_But that won’t count for diddly squat if he doesn’t have the resources to fight back against partisan attacks. They know Rob’s a threat, and they’ll spend millions to try to stop him._
But going head to head with these partisans and the special interests backing them won’t be easy, and it ain’t going to be cheap, either.
**[So will you help Rob’s campaign get their fundraising back on track by splitting a $25 donation with Heartland Patriots today? We’ve got a chance to do something pretty special in Iowa, but it’s going to take every one of us to pull off a big upset.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93343?refcode=em20250808-9237&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=9237%2E614464%2ENIdLPc)**

name: Navy Seal Adam Schwarze
subject: They think we’re assholes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
This woman may not technically speak for the Democrat Party, but she has captured the essence of what her fellow members of Congress believe. | [](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=d70ee43becbf4507a44446db350d0091&_e=UEHszLQMOA7OpKByEEW5VCRLKbRE_4K3OU1QkBIcnnLu7uVHCxHwLQ6QoT1c9UK0JuCgaToe9WqiQBUmrICbiNFgaWf6m4d3tabzyKGaawklyNX9inkmELyYmHuBYbHh4G8zpB_pYDpFVigLZyTlE4ZnPnttmpWJO2CLUTcBLNMLUi_7lzyEq925jn2lzWpNqZKtIN6r5GOlXhfjth9LagBz5mxA76H6hSUvKddw0CmNYdL0irSG2vAp3KHAvvg7tPd8F2U-4egx62_vOUCaLc9THsrd4EfAb-pFspzkH-Gp_lwfe9h24oQWDixttypvgHD9pyNoFiI_U3B2vqYGNhrkbNmQQvQm1CUd30WImWAoVv6QR39VvUgM3EXZt4ns0vFHBXp-GcwbQ9tfUzRn0nG1yCQIp3Tx_696TRFJiW4utUxZ3z6gqMXYqdAF9aZx8e3W7v58U7mfhGmoO_8_6vbKBuWdS_9GXBrDoraqZC7t1EXPsYdFegWfrLkDeA-ajm06766oQjZEeuNn4umDi2P0UNOSdt6SNl-t5Yq35xBTUSxQSBTrWoLDiuGvkyPCCiA2MPqvYlTY6FRtcwkDQJN7CFGF4KtVVJR7JfvVTLSYrivjSTBE5y6-MnqsFLmE-yTfDHK3z8PZHHS9H6-p7ycgaGVKXkv1Uu3AY77LMFnd9uwpE2vI00bHQ76TE8Nwfc_Wk8dYGnKjoBuXl72o05dOKw_T_iRW98ICSPEXnkZYxMk_DZobYrYuh_-ohmkJ)

---
Fellow Conservative,

_**“Asshole. Buffoon. Mofo. Wannabe Hitler. Gangster. Vile villain.”**_

Those are just a few words Rep. Jasmine Crockett used to describe President Trump. But let’s be honest: those insults aren’t just about him. They’re about you. About me. About anyone who leans conservative, wears a uniform, or believes in this country’s founding values.

They think we’re backward. They believe we are in the way. And worst of all, they think we’ll take it.

**As a former Navy SEAL Officer and Marine Corps veteran, I’ve faced far worse than name-calling. I’ve deployed to over 70 countries. I’ve led warriors into battle. I’ve endured life-threatening missions.** And the most important lesson I’ve learned? Quiet professionalism always wins the day.

_[](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=d70ee43becbf4507a44446db350d0091&_e=UEHszLQMOA7OpKByEEW5VCRLKbRE_4K3OU1QkBIcnnLu7uVHCxHwLQ6QoT1c9UK0JuCgaToe9WqiQBUmrICbiNFgaWf6m4d3tabzyKGaawklyNX9inkmELyYmHuBYbHh4G8zpB_pYDpFVigLZyTlE4ZnPnttmpWJO2CLUTcBLNMLUi_7lzyEq925jn2lzWpNqZKtIN6r5GOlXhfjth9LagBz5mxA76H6hSUvKddw0CmNYdL0irSG2vAp3KHAvvg7tPd8F2U-4egx62_vOUCaLVIarde7TXPYCy9S3x9uv7yBky3JjkhG74bnl9j7STyotGK7TdjSOPuAIwO05WhUXv_5HGVR9nlp0B5zX1sxyDlXu-D3L-st3ptLbU5rjx6hvyayDmZ9x7BK3SBhxk2-SFmp4BfFXGOShOo0ijpyqq-pQFFn_LTYZABmeQ-eXLSeq1tMnzsR6zKIIKc-xhY3MQFWL_s_Xdxtg-k3iqdhGstwMfGYNJKCARQGXgGwBSyzL6eTC8ZcLSQzVvcOmcVsapyHqZmQJvad8k9Xqblyp2ol0R7an5UwmKyP_0hATXmWPVlNbshem9zhu0yn3imoOLXrnSzy2Q9u0f_qFITGdQUXyIsDAgzmakpIzn1P_gaKc7iv1z4jkDNdqzgOmujFoTevRhm9s1NSDayHMZTlXptX8qWWRapXoP9MOO5QbAhbGF5yeGX8tsQ8O5i7QVxV5ZSSasvaN2-2FpvUEHOA9SrKBdOx0hp9i6McDyKqTqDG)_
That’s why I’m running for U.S. Senate in Minnesota — not to yell or mock, but to**fight with discipline, humility, and relentless purpose.**

I didn’t plan on entering politics. But the more I came home from deployment, the more I saw a Minnesota I didn’t recognize. Tim Walz’s lieutenant governor — the person I’m running against — isn’t just radically out of step with our values; she embodies the same hard-left extremism that’s trying to rewrite our entire society.

While Walz called for calm in 2020, she was preparing a lawsuit against the Minneapolis Police. As crime soared and businesses burned, she blamed the people trying to hold the line. These are the people in charge now — and they’re not moderating.

**You can close this email and forget my name. Or you can help send the second Navy SEAL to the U.S. Senate — someone trained for chaos, unafraid of the fight, and grounded in service, not ego. **

clean_body: This woman may not technically speak for the Democrat Party, but she has captured the essence of what her fellow members of Congress believe. | Fellow Conservative, “Asshole. Buffoon. Mofo. Wannabe Hitler. Gangster. Vile villain.” Those are just a few words Rep. Jasmine Crockett used to describe President Trump. But let’s be honest: those insults aren’t just about him. They’re about you. About me. About anyone who leans conservative, wears a uniform, or believes in this country’s founding values. They think we’re backward. They believe we are in the way. And worst of all, they think we’ll take it. As a former Navy SEAL Officer and Marine Corps veteran, I’ve faced far worse than name-calling. I’ve deployed to over 70 countries. I’ve led warriors into battle. I’ve endured life-threatening missions. And the most important lesson I’ve learned? Quiet professionalism always wins the day. That’s why I’m running for U.S. Senate in Minnesota — not to yell or mock, but tofight with discipline, humility, and relentless purpose. I didn’t plan on entering politics. But the more I came home from deployment, the more I saw a Minnesota I didn’t recognize. Tim Walz’s lieutenant governor — the person I’m running against — isn’t just radically out of step with our values; she embodies the same hard-left extremism that’s trying to rewrite our entire society. While Walz called for calm in 2020, she was preparing a lawsuit against the Minneapolis Police. As crime soared and businesses burned, she blamed the people trying to hold the line. These are the people in charge now — and they’re not moderating. You can close this email and forget my name. Or you can help send the second Navy SEAL to the U.S. Senate — someone trained for chaos, unafraid of the fight, and grounded in service, not ego. I grew up playing hockey. I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday in October 2001, less than two months after 9/11. I served because I love this country, wanted to make my mom proud, and knew I had something to give. Twenty-four years later, I still feel that calling. It’s just a different kind of battlefield. This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t gone red in a presidential election since 1976. And I know I’m the underdog — just a regular guy from Minnesota who’s been asked to do extraordinary things one more time. With your help, I know we can flip this Senate seat for the Republican Party - but that will require you getting involved TODAY. And you have a choice. I’ve earned advanced degrees from Harvard and Johns Hopkins. I’ve trained in nuclear deterrence. I’ve worked across cultures, built teams under pressure, and led calmly when it mattered most. But none of that compares to the challenge ahead of me now — earning your trust and building a movement powerful enough to flip this seat. I’m not asking for a blank check. I’m asking for a chance. Because we still live in a country worth defending. Respect and results still matter. And I believe people like you are ready to fight — not with insults, but with action. For a better tomorrow, Adam Schwarze Retired Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. name: Solar Go-Fridge
subject: New Solar-Powered King of Coolers
body:
"... Tax foreign nations, not American citizens!"
| | | | We are a community of gun-loving Americans. We hope you stick around but remember you can [unsubscribe](https://app.gunowner-news.com/campaigns/nl231exsbxaed/track-url/pa6499axa53b5/58df5769ab42b3ecf9384f7cacdb8e4c43463a43) here at any time.

_
Bringing you the world of gun news in America._
|
---
New Solar-Powered King of Coolers | **NEW Solar-Powered
"King of Coolers"
Never Needs Ice!** [](https://app.gunowner-news.com/campaigns/nl231exsbxaed/track-url/pa6499axa53b5/3f98346fda01d1582f5db54a2e1cd66fd0bc3601) **No More Soggy Food!** This revolutionary fridge on wheels keeps your food and drinks ice cold... **But it NEVER needs ice!** It may look like an ordinary cooler, but you've never seen anything like this... Instead of ice, it uses advanced technology to keep all your important food and drinks ice cold. So you'll never have to stop for ice every couple of hours... And you'll never have to dip your hands into soggy food or gross puddles of water. And since you'll never need ice, you'll have all that precious cooler space available for MORE of your favorite food and drinks. **And it's super flexible.** This revolutionary Solar Go-Fridge is **not JUST a cooler - it's a refrigerator, freezer, and deep freezer** all in one! It can work as a fridge for hot dogs and drinks, or as a freezer for ice cream, popsicles and frozen meats... And it can even go down to subzero temperatures! **The Solar Go-Fridge
Charges For FREE With
The Power of the Sun!** Unlike other run-of-the-mill coolers, your Solar Go-Fridge... **Charges for FREE in the Sun!** Yes, you can recharge your Solar Go-Fridge using the FREE power of the sun. After all, we couldn't call it a "Solar" Go-Fridge if it wasn't solar powered... And that's why... **You get a FREE 40-Watt Solar Panel with your Solar Go-Fridge.** [](https://app.gunowner-news.com/campaigns/nl231exsbxaed/track-url/pa6499axa53b5/3f98346fda01d1582f5db54a2e1cd66fd0bc3601) You can use your solar panel to power your Solar Go-Fridge on the go... So you can take it to campsites, sporting events, RV trips, tailgates, backyard BBQs, pool parties and so much more... Or use it to power your critical devices. It's like a mini power cell in its own right. It works with phones, tablets and your other critical electronic devices. Plus, if a tree falls on some power lines and cuts the power to your neighborhood, you don't have to worry about the food in your fridge spoiling. **Look What You Can Fit
In Your Solar Go-Fridge** Your Solar Go-Fridge can take any kind of frozen supplies with you. And when we say _any kind of supplies_ , we mean it. It has a small footprint, but has spacious capacity. **You get 42 quarts of space** for your critical items - that's like 46 cans or bottles of water. And no bulky ice bags! [](https://app.gunowner-news.com/campaigns/nl231exsbxaed/track-url/pa6499axa53b5/3f98346fda01d1582f5db54a2e1cd66fd0bc3601) Its compact size and all-terrain wheels make it easy to throw in the trunk. Or to bring with you to tailgates. It's truly small, but mighty. Drinks, hot dogs, medicine and more. It can all fit in your Solar Go-Fridge. **[Limited Time] Get
Exclusive Introductory
Pricing, Plus 11 Bonus
Gifts Worth $388 And
FREE Shipping!** Order now to take advantage of our special introductory pricing, 11 free bonus gifts worth $388, and free shipping. Your exclusive 11 FREE Bonus Gifts package includes:
* **FREE** 40-Watt Solar Panel
* **FREE** 72-Hour Survival Food Kit
* **FREE** Survival Coffee
* **FREE** Survival Cocoa
* **FREE** StarFire Camp Stove
* **FREE** Freedom Flame
* **FREE** Multi-Use Flashlight
* **FREE** Solar Power Digital Report
* **FREE** $25 Certificate
* **FREE** Shipping & Handling
* **FREE** VIP Phone Line

Plus there's an easy payment plan available with no credit check... [**Order Now To Lock In Your
Low Pricing, FREE Bonus
Gifts and FREE Shipping!**](https://app.gunowner-news.com/campaigns/nl231exsbxaed/track-url/pa6499axa53b5/3f98346fda01d1582f5db54a2e1cd66fd0bc3601) [](https://app.gunowner-news.com/campaigns/nl231exsbxaed/track-url/pa6499axa53b5/3f98346fda01d1582f5db54a2e1cd66fd0bc3601) **But Hurry... Last Time
The Solar Go-Fridge
SOLD OUT In 24 Hours** **There is one catch:** We can't reserve any Solar Go-Fridges or give out any IOUs. You'll need to ACT FAST... Because the last version of the Solar Go-Fridge sold out in 24 hours after only a couple of text messages... And that version couldn't do half the things this NEW model can. So when people find out about this space-age ice chest, there could be a mad rush. **So don't wait...** If you miss out on this special Introductory Pricing, there's nothing we can do. There's only one way to guarantee your Introductory Pricing, FREE gifts and FREE shipping... And that's by clicking the link below to grab your Solar Go-Fridge while they're in stock and ready to ship: [**Order Now To Lock In Your
Low Pricing, FREE Bonus
Gifts and FREE Shipping!**](https://app.gunowner-news.com/campaigns/nl231exsbxaed/track-url/pa6499axa53b5/3f98346fda01d1582f5db54a2e1cd66fd0bc3601) So make sure to grab yours now. **I know you'll love it!** Stay safe and have fun out there,

Frank Bates
Patriot Headquarters P.S. Thanks to your support, we're able to provide support to important charities that help American veterans and their families. Charities like Fisher House, A Soldier's Child Foundation, Team Rubicon and Operation Homefront. **Customers Are
Raving About Their
Solar Go-Fridge** **Kristen G.**
"It was super fun to use. We were gone for four nights, and didn't have to get ice at all. Not having to deal with ice the whole time was amazing. I was worried that it wasn't big enough. But it held absolutely everything I was planning to take because I didn't have to put any ice in there. It didn't have to be as big as my regular cooler." **Randy M.**
"You cannot beat it. It is awesome. No running around, looking for drinks. No more opening up your drink and setting it on the tractor and coming back 20 minutes later and it's warm. Everything's cold. Everything's cold all the time. Just a wonderful, wonderful unit. I am a Type 1 diabetic. It's been keeping my insulin cold to the point where I don't worry about my insulin. I know it's good." **Damon H.**
"We all have lots of coolers. But this is a cooler extraordinaire. Not only is it a cooler, it's a fully-functioning refrigerator. It's great for me being a fisherman and an outdoor guy. I can put a bunch of fish in here and set it at freezing temperatures. And when I get home, my fish will stay frozen and I don't need any ice. It's a great tool. It's absolutely a must for campers and fisherman. It looks like a cooler, but it's actually a refrigerator which makes it a really special thing." **Lisa W.**
"You don't have to stop and get ice anymore. That was one thing I hated to do early in the morning when we had to leave to go out of town to play in a baseball tournament. We had to leave at 5 or 6 or sometimes 4:30 in the morning and had to stop to get ice for our cooler. So this is nice because you just have to set it on refrigerator mode and you get your cold drinks or whatever you put in your cooler. People are already taking pictures with it, trying to find out more information so they could get their own." **Lou H.**
"Please get one or two of them like I did. They will come in handy next time you have a power failure or going on a trip. I use mine whenever we travel or recently when we had a power failure a few weeks ago. You won't be sorry." [**Order Now To Lock In Your
Low Pricing, FREE Bonus
Gifts and FREE Shipping!**](https://app.gunowner-news.com/campaigns/nl231exsbxaed/track-url/pa6499axa53b5/3f98346fda01d1582f5db54a2e1cd66fd0bc3601) [](https://app.gunowner-news.com/campaigns/nl231exsbxaed/track-url/pa6499axa53b5/3f98346fda01d1582f5db54a2e1cd66fd0bc3601)

We were gone for four nights, and didn't have to get ice at all. Not having to deal with ice the whole time was amazing. I was worried that it wasn't big enough. But it held absolutely everything I was planning to take because I didn't have to put any ice in there. It didn't have to be as big as my regular cooler." Randy M. "You cannot beat it. It is awesome. No running around, looking for drinks. No more opening up your drink and setting it on the tractor and coming back 20 minutes later and it's warm. Everything's cold. Everything's cold all the time. Just a wonderful, wonderful unit. I am a Type 1 diabetic. It's been keeping my insulin cold to the point where I don't worry about my insulin. I know it's good." Damon H. "We all have lots of coolers. But this is a cooler extraordinaire. Not only is it a cooler, it's a fully-functioning refrigerator. It's great for me being a fisherman and an outdoor guy. I can put a bunch of fish in here and set it at freezing temperatures. And when I get home, my fish will stay frozen and I don't need any ice. It's a great tool. It's absolutely a must for campers and fisherman. It looks like a cooler, but it's actually a refrigerator which makes it a really special thing." Lisa W. "You don't have to stop and get ice anymore. That was one thing I hated to do early in the morning when we had to leave to go out of town to play in a baseball tournament. We had to leave at 5 or 6 or sometimes 4:30 in the morning and had to stop to get ice for our cooler. So this is nice because you just have to set it on refrigerator mode and you get your cold drinks or whatever you put in your cooler. People are already taking pictures with it, trying to find out more information so they could get their own." Lou H.** "Please get one or two of them like I did. They will come in handy next time you have a power failure or going on a trip. I use mine whenever we travel or recently when we had a power failure a few weeks ago. You won't be sorry." | We are a community of gun-loving Americans. We hope you stick around but remember you can here at any time. | | |

name: Bryan
subject: Radicals Leave Coffin at My Home
body:
Recently, Left wing protestors left a full-size coffin outside my door...

| | | | | [|
---](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=95f6ed3a4d&e=e907534017)
| | | | | _In case you missed it..._
---
| | | | | Recently, Left-wing protesters held a procession [**outside my home**](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=d6d0b8c1c8&e=e907534017) with an upside-down American flag — leaving behind a full-size coffin at my front door.
---
[|
---](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=f7e2e1e68d&e=e907534017)
| This follows my vote to stand with President Trump to:
✅ Secure our Border✅ Make the Trump Tax Cuts Permanent✅ Eliminate Waste, Fraud, and Abuse in Medicaid
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=0779aa50be&e=e907534017)
| | It’s disappointing that Democrat leadership and the radical Left are resorting to these types of tactics.
In fact, [**reports**](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=2549878cfb&e=e907534017)**** have highlighted that these same individuals chose to attend my town hall, attempt to disrupt the event, and even [**boo the Pledge of Allegiance**](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=ae823e794f&e=e907534017).
---
| Their [**radical policies and extreme rhetoric will backfire**](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=64feae07e3&e=e907534017) , but I need your help to make sure we get our message out.
---
| [FIGHT BACK AGAINST RADICALISM](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=54be724f52&e=e907534017)
---
| _**Socialists like A.O.C and Bernie Sanders are raising**_[ _**millions of dollars**_](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=384e443497&e=e907534017) _**—**_ and it’s clear that I am one of the Left’s [top targets](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=934989c784&e=e907534017).
Last cycle, the left spent **$4.4 million** against us. With Donald Trump in the White House, they’re sure to do so again.
---
| [_**Will you stand with me and fight back against the radicals by donating $15, $25, or $50 today?**_](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=4e4780b352&e=e907534017)
Together we can fight back and bring integrity back to politics.
---

name: Sean Gregory
subject: MAGA money floods Nassau
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Koslow for Nassau
body:
This just dropped – Bruce Blakeman is calling his upstate friends to help flood this race with national MAGA money. | | Friends,

This just dropped – Bruce Blakeman is calling his upstate friends to help flood this race with **national MAGA money**. **[We need supporters like you to rush $10, $20, or whatever you can give right now to push back against the tide of extreme MAGA money coming in from across the country.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112969403/562208061/-1414184198?contactdata=wPLy9WTEYrkwn+RzL/qV61O2XWoEN2eJDBI8Vp5AXGBu5oTjqjDgHdh6x3jIm1boOlXf42jt8t3WeetgcWVtfd5AQ6Ut8+cZuBkjwGjZN3c=&am=20.00&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTYyMTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZGY4MzBmZmEtNmI3NC1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=bRitF21YcGjizs_wjNN-ExTxZaGOAiwcK4WReW0ktbA=&emci=bc7ea5fa-5d74-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=df830ffa-6b74-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=17551891)**

**Elise Stefanik – who knows far more about the Adirondacks than Nassau County – just began fundraising for Blakeman.** She’s called Blakeman a “proven leader” and “critical to keeping Nassau County true to our values.”

We in Nassau have seen the “values” Blakeman’s governance has had here in the community; bloated government spending that benefits his friends and cronies and screws us, the taxpayers.

[**We need an executive that will stand up for Nassau and help families across the county, but we can’t do it alone. | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112970384/562210975/-727799932?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_HashmiSplit_250808_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2JhYWZmOC02ZDc0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=p6fwPhuXgrVE7OXzutlqGXT2bcVBy2pXERoqrUF1j3A=) Derek, I have a simple but crucial request for you today:

[_**With less than three months to go until Election Day, will you split a contribution of $10, $20, or anything you can afford between Ghazala Hashmi’s campaign for Lt. Governor of Virginia and my reelection campaign?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112970385/562210976/-727799932?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_HashmiSplit_250808_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2JhYWZmOC02ZDc0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=p6fwPhuXgrVE7OXzutlqGXT2bcVBy2pXERoqrUF1j3A=)

With Congressional Republicans embracing Trump’s chaotic, dangerous agenda at every turn, we need leaders in Virginia who are committed to fighting back against their corruption and protecting the Commonwealth’s working families.

**Ghazala has been doing exactly that for the past five years in the Virginia senate – I’ve witnessed it firsthand. I’m more than confident we can trust her to be a staunch advocate for our rights and freedoms as our next Lt. Governor.**

Why? She has consistently championed legislation to improve the lives of Virginians – focusing on protecting contraception and reproductive freedom, increasing access to affordable education, standing up for health care and environmental protections, and much more. Not to mention, in a closely divided Virginia Senate, Ghazala could be the tie-breaking vote on critical legislation. That’s why flipping this office from red to blue is so important.

Unfortunately, Republicans will do whatever it takes to stop Ghazala from flipping this seat so they can expand their power in Virginia.

[_**So please, Derek, if you can, will you split a donation between our campaigns right now?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112970386/562210977/-727799932?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_HashmiSplit_250808_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2JhYWZmOC02ZDc0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=p6fwPhuXgrVE7OXzutlqGXT2bcVBy2pXERoqrUF1j3A=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be automatically split between Ghazala Hashmi and Mark Warner:_ **[EXPRESS DONATE clean_body: | | | = Derek, I have a simple but crucial request for you today: = With Congressional Republicans embracing Trump’s chaotic, dangerous agenda at every turn, we need leaders in Virginia who are committed to fighting back against their corruption and protecting the Commonwealth’s working families. Ghazala has been doing exactly that for the past five years in the Virginia senate – I’ve witnessed it firsthand. I’m more than confident we can trust her to be a staunch advocate for our rights and freedoms as our next Lt. Governor. Why? She has consistently championed legislation to improve the lives of Virginians – focusing on protecting contraception and reproductive freedom, increasing access to affordable education, standing up for health care and environmental protections, and much more. Not to mention, in a closely divided Virginia Senate, Ghazala could be the tie-breaking vote on critical legislation. That’s why flipping this office from red to blue is so important. Unfortunately, Republicans will do whatever it takes to stop Ghazala from flipping this seat so they can expand their power in Virginia. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be automatically split between Ghazala Hashmi and Mark Warner: = = = = = = Thank you, Mark Warner **= | | Friends of Mark Warner 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States | Click here to . | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner

body:
| | | Hi friend, it's Marc Elias. **I beat Donald Trump 64 times in court. And now, working with the DSCC,_I'll beat him again._**

[**Before I tell you more, will you chip in $5 to the DSCC to help power the work to stop Trump's allies and take back the Senate majority? >>**](https://go.dscc.org/go/21844?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250808-MarcElias-EliasSuingTrump-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250808-MarcElias-EliasSuingTrump-Pros-sl1&akid=20942%2E9716520%2ErCW4aV)

Right now, Donald Trump is attacking our longstanding values and principles. He's ignoring due process, flaunting anti-corruption laws, and, worst of all, **attacking our democracy and voting rights -- the very bedrock of American society.**

It's disgusting, but not surprising from the guy who claimed he wanted to be a dictator on his first day in office.

That's where my team and the DSCC come in -- the very moment Trump signed an executive order to interfere in our elections and threaten the rights of voters, we _immediately_ filed suit against him on behalf of Democrats across the nation. And we've already notched some key victories in the case!

**But the fight isn't over.** We all know that Trump and his allies will keep trying to break American democracy. We can't let them, so we need ALL Democrats -- from leaders like Barack and Michelle Obama to supporters like you -- working together.

**So I'm asking for your help as we work to stop Trump, take back the Senate, and defend democracy:[Please, will you donate $5 or more today to support the DSCC's work and take back the Senate?](https://go.dscc.org/go/21844?amount=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250808-MarcElias-EliasSuingTrump-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250808-MarcElias-EliasSuingTrump-Pros-sl1&akid=20942%2E9716520%2ErCW4aV)**

name: New PA Project Education Fund
subject: Less than 3 months until Election Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund
body:
<a clicktracking=off href="http://example.com">
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/NPP_Ed_logo_RGB_green1-01.jpg]
Hi Friend,
Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter!
Earlier this week, the PA [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] House State Government Committee [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house.
An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills.
HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.”
State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [https://www.politicspa.com/state-government-committee-sends-voter-id-election-omnibus-bills-to-house-floor/141399] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.”
HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.”
Among other changes, [https://penncapital-star.com/campaigns-elections/voter-id-and-vote-by-mail-reform-bills-move-to-pa-house] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers.
The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed.
The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error.
Upcoming events
As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Pittsburgh%20School%20board%20forum%208.5x11.png]
Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students.
Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader.
With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color.
When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793662/542679746/-435660973]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Allegheny%20judicial%20flyer.png]
Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot.
Your vote shapes our justice system!
Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager
Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms.
When: Monday, May 12, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793661/542679745/229023784]
Social Media of the Week
https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/ [https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/]
The deadline to request your mail-in ballot for the primary election is May 13th at 5PM.
Request a mail-in ballot or find your local ballot drop-off location here [vote.pa]
Your county election office must receive your completed mail ballot by 8PM on May 20th.
We recommend mailing it at least 5 days prior, or delivering it by hand at an official ballot drop off location.
#GoVotePA
SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/]
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/7237344C447445787878394747342B75584E47375A57552F745265427662334B544A4A36505A58657A5871474B7A5A6E53705571424758325657365969365747/editor_images/WCM%20logo.JPG] #CourtsMatter
This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [https://www.democracydocket.com/news-alerts/a-double-win-for-voters-in-pennsylvania] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls.
First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/445-2025-04-24-Order-denying-PA-Attorney-Generals-motion-to-intervene.pdf] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots.
The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/cases/pennsylvania-undated-and-wrongly-dated-mail-in-ballots-challenge-eakin/] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted.
After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/441-2025-04-02-Notice-Of-Appeal-.pdf] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary.
Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as Citizen_AG, filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/1-2024-10-29-Complaint.pdf] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act.
The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls.
District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/67-2025-05-02-Memorandum-.pdf] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place.
In law, standing [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standing_(law)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case.
In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money]
The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests.
In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money] .
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/DelCo_2.png] We are hiring!
Let's provide civic education together!
We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania.
Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote!
Positions start at $20 an hour.
Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org]
EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org]
News You Can Use
There are things every one of us can do [https://www.democracydocket.com/opinion/we-have-more-power-than-trump-wants-us-to-believe-heres-what-you-can-do/] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last.
1. Educate Yourself
Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally.
Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more.
* New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pittsburgh-mayor-election-2025-candidates-quiz]
* Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections/assistant/]
* Everything you need to know about mail voting [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-mail-ballot-how-to-request-fill-out-return] , becoming a poll worker [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide]
* Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pennsylvania-primary-election-day-2025-voter-information-registration]
* Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-commonwealth-superior-court-candidates] , vetting judicial candidates [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-candidate-vetting-guide]
* Guides to State College school board [https://www.spotlightpa.org/statecollege/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-state-college-school-board-anderson-huff-barlow-black-desmarais-mcternan-sogor] , Reading City Council president [https://www.spotlightpa.org/berks/2025/04/reading-city-council-president-primary-election-2025]
* Guides to voting as a college student [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-college-student-voting-guide]
En Español
* Guía para votar por correo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pensilvania-elecciones-primarias-2025-votar-correo-como-llenar] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/reading-concejo-municipal-primarias-elecciones-2025]
* Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-primarias-elecciones-2025-votar-informacion] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-elecciones-2025-como-convertirse-trabajador-electoral]
2. Stay Engaged
To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win.
Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [https://www.inquirer.com/politics/pennsylvania/montgomery-county-commissioner-neil-makhija-emmy-20250508.html] )
3. Share What You Learn
Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting.
Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year!
4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations
Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work.
Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above!
5. Become a Trained Poll Worker
Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process.
Here are some resources that may be helpful:
Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [https://penncapital-star.com/briefs/advocacy-groups-offer-training-for-pa-primary-poll-watchers/]
Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide/]
Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [https://fcfreepresspa.com/poll-workers-needed-for-pennsylvanias-2025-primary-election/]
6. Engage Your Elected Officials
Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise.
7. Vote in Every Election
Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility.
Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th!

This event was a declaration that Pennsylvania stands against injustice and division.

clean_body: [] Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter! Earlier this week, the PA [] House State Government Committee [] [] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house. An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills. HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.” State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.” HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.” Among other changes, [] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers. The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed. The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error. Upcoming events As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls. [] Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students. Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader. With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color. When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 RSVP [] [] Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot. Your vote shapes our justice system! Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms. When: Monday, May 12, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 RSVP [] Social Media of the Week [] The deadline to request your mail-in ballot for the primary election is May 13th at 5PM. Request a mail-in ballot or find your local ballot drop-off location here [vote.pa] Your county election office must receive your completed mail ballot by 8PM on May 20th. We recommend mailing it at least 5 days prior, or delivering it by hand at an official ballot drop off location. #GoVotePA SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [] [] #CourtsMatter This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls. First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots. The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted. After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary. Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as CitizenAG, filed [] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act. The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls. District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place. In law, standing [)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case. In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [] The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests. In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [] . [] We are hiring! Let's provide civic education together! We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania. Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote! Positions start at $20 an hour. Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org] EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org] News You Can Use There are things every one of us can do [] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last. 1. Stay Engaged To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win. Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [] ) 3. # FRIDAY, AUGUST 8, 2025
---|---
**Good Afternoon!** Legacy media has failed America. Only **31% of Americans** said they trust the mainstream news. Spin, bias, and hidden agendas have driven millions of people to simply tune out, tired of being misled by outlets that put profit and politics ahead of the truth. At _The Flyover_ , we're proving there's a better way. And you now have an opportunity to [invest as we grow it](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=08b58df27d2b4746a8c42d640629855a&_e=15tD4oxvCk955WrcF6jRkuq44yBJT9u76C5hhGedu0DI3qEGg7noc0xBDihxSVrAaQYuRBsIIy7G9POXfL6oX4-0iht2V4ao4TCx1v0iq7AZ0Qrp0ZktRiP83Ost-C4-bGsWRI7NKmb9J7eRMJxBzo6JCvA0kswkNMcDQW8h3if0A1iNRUyvf9mJBhKfaBVZeSQ4VN_1rYdZRw-BVG2cPC3a66HDcK93GdaJMAS45y23CgUqWvv4vPZpb1TuOeV0Xq7lbXuTkI6tlsVz0jpIMsvb03gPEaD4SQLTtrvcu7VKBSyLHfw0od8lhzGCbiIhbUM5DFDup_QV9RafvSJpVmBxkfUgKov9Wa5dI0jzODU%3D). In just two years, we've built a community of **2.5 million loyal readers** who turn to _The Flyover_ for honest, fact-based news they can count on. With **611% year-over-year revenue growth** and engagement metrics that leave legacy outlets in the dust, we've shown there's real demand for news that respects readers instead of manipulating them. Since day one, my priority has always been to use my voice and vote to help our community. This is just some of my recent work in Congress:
* I just started the BIOTech Caucus, building on the release of the National Security Commission on Emerging Biotechnology’s recent report to Congress, to raise awareness of the strength of other countries (like China) and to promote solutions that foster domestic innovation and improve our national security. More on that in an upcoming newsletter.

* As co-chair of the Climate Solutions Caucus, I fought against elimination of the EV and other clean energy tax credits in the Republican budget bill and am working on the upcoming Farm Bill to support local farmers while also promoting conservation.

* I continue to push paid family leave policies at the federal level, most recently introducing the More Paid Leave for More Americans Act to take advantage of the patchwork of successful state-level programs across the country.

* I wrote the Public Service Loan Forgiveness Payment Completion Fairness Act to make it easier for people in public service careers to have their student loans forgiven.

**Every day, I'm working hard to build relationships with colleagues to deliver on issues that matter most to you and your family, from making health care more affordable and accessible to enabling economic growth._But I can't continue this work without your critical grassroots support, Derek. _**

With GOP lawmakers in Washington doing anything the Trump administration wants them to do – gutting Medicaid and SNAP, defunding PBS, shutting down the House so they don’t have to vote on releasing the Epstein files – it's absolutely critical for us to protect every single Democratic seat. And that includes Chrissy's. The last thing we need right now is yet another Republican rubber stamp in Washington for our President’s reckless and dangerous agenda. = | | = Thank you, Michelle Dorothy, Chief Political Advisor \---------- Forwarded message ---------- From: Chrissy Date: Thu, Jul 17, 2025 Subject: URGENT: Wealthy Republican businessman files candidacy for PA-06 | | | I needed to share this update with you directly, Derek:A wealthy Republican businessman just filed paperwork to run against me. With the GOP in control of the White House, the Senate, and the House, it's absolutely critical for Democrats to hold on to every seat possible – including mine in PA-06 – to more effectively push back against the reckless and dangerous actions of this administration. We can't wait until next year to take action, Derek. Building a strong grassroots movement must start now so we're prepared as we head into another election year in 2026. That's why I'm personally asking: = | | = Since day one, my priority has always been to use my voice and vote to help our community. This is just some of my recent work in Congress: I just started the BIOTech Caucus, building on the release of the National Security Commission on Emerging Biotechnology’s recent report to Congress, to raise awareness of the strength of other countries (like China) and to promote solutions that foster domestic innovation and improve our national security. More on that in an upcoming newsletter. As co-chair of the Climate Solutions Caucus, I fought against elimination of the EV and other clean energy tax credits in the Republican budget bill and am working on the upcoming Farm Bill to support local farmers while also promoting conservation. I continue to push paid family leave policies at the federal level, most recently introducing the More Paid Leave for More Americans Act to take advantage of the patchwork of successful state-level programs across the country. I wrote the Public Service Loan Forgiveness Payment Completion Fairness Act to make it easier for people in public service careers to have their student loans forgiven. Every day, I'm working hard to build relationships with colleagues to deliver on issues that matter most to you and your family, from making health care more affordable and accessible to enabling economic growth.But I can't continue this work without your critical grassroots support, Derek. = | | = There's work to be done, Chrissy | Chrissy Houlahan for Congress P.O. Box 222 Devon, PA 19333 United States Emails are a crucial way for us to stay in touch with our top supporters; however, we understand if you need a break from political emails right now. If you'd like to pause emails for the next few weeks, you can click . If you'd like to receive fewer emails like this one, or if you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please Chrissy is a former Captain in the United States Air Force. Use of her military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or Department of the Air Force. Paid for by Chrissy Houlahan for Congress. Chrissy Houlahan for Congress is only soliciting donations of up to $3,300 per election from individuals and other federally permissible sources. Chrissy Houlahan for Congress is not soliciting any funds in excess of that amount or any funds from corporations, labor organizations, or any federally prohibited sources. |

name: Eleni Kounalakis
subject: I have big news to share
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eleni Kounalakis for Treasurer 2026
body:
https://www.efundraisingconnections.com/c/EleniKounalakisTreas?refcode=logo [https://www.efundraisingconnections.com/c/EleniKounalakisTreas?refcode=logo]
Peter,

As a supporter of my campaign for Governor of California, I wanted to write to you first with some news: After careful consideration and reflection, I have made the decision to run instead for State Treasurer in 2026.

First and foremost, I want to thank you for your support over the past few months. Your generosity and trust in my ability to lead means the world to me - and I’m asking you to stick with me as I set out on this new, exciting path.

Traveling our state and meeting with Californians of all walks of life, I am convinced that the best way I can serve our state is as Treasurer. I will bring a deep commitment to fiscal responsibility, economic opportunity, and strategic investment in our state’s priorities — from affordable housing and clean energy to infrastructure and education.

My commitment to fighting for the California Dream will never wane, and it is centered on making it affordable to live in our state. That starts with housing. With nearly 20 years experience in the housing sector, I will apply my skills every day to efficiently leverage every dollar at our disposal to increase housing production. This work is central to the office of the State Treasurer and I am prepared and excited for the challenge.

I know this is a big change. But I also know it is the right one. I am incredibly grateful for your trust and energy, and I hope you will join me in this new effort.

-Eleni Kounalakis

DONATE [https://www.efundraisingconnections.com/c/EleniKounalakisTreas?refcode2=84c2d973-7174-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b]

San Francisco, CA 94115

## Start your day in the know
Are you starting you day in the know? Every morning at 9am ET you can join the Morning Meeting with myself, Mark Halperin and Dan Turrentine. We walk thru the news of the day like no one else and then take your questions. You can find it all on the same [YouTube channel](https://substack.com/redirect/a1ca5c85-6b0d-4a0c-af9f-9bc1425a7000?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as my 6pm show. Make sure to subscribe and hit the notification button so you don't miss it!
[Subscribe HERE](https://substack.com/redirect/a1ca5c85-6b0d-4a0c-af9f-9bc1425a7000?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
## Next week on The Sean Spicer Show...
We've got a great line-up next week, starting Monday with Breitbart's editor-in-chief Alex Marlow. Wednesday, I'm talking with journalist John Solomon about the latest on the Russia Hoax. Finally, we've got The Trafalgar Group's Robert Cahaly to finish the week strong with the latest polling data. Turn on notifications so you don't miss it!
## Wellness update...
If you watch the show or have been reading the newsletter for a while you know I have been on my own MAHA/wellness journey. One of the big things I have done is switched out many of the personal care products (soap, toothpaste, vitamins, etc) and home care products (cleaning supplies, detergent) with products from an American based, family company where wellness is front and center.
If you are interested in finding out what I did, text INFO to 276-500-1185 and join me!
