name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
subject: AIPAC is attacking me
body:
God commands me to walk in truth
| | | | Friend,
The floodgates have opened, I’m officially under attack from AIPAC for for saying, **“the most truthful and easiest thing to say that Oct 7th in Israel was horrific and all hostages must be returned, but so is the genocide, humanitarian crisis, and starvation happening in Gaza.”**
The genocide, humanitarian crisis, and starvation in Gaza aren’t political issues for me, they are issues of my Christian faith. **God commands me to walk in truth and love.**
He commands me to walk in truth of the Gospel, that Jesus was fully divine and fully human, and who died for our sins and was resurrected in victory over death.
He also commands me to walk in love, by obeying His commandments, including to love one another.
What is happening in Gaza is a violation of God’s commandments and a travesty of humanity, and I refuse to bear false witness for my own political benefit.
What is political about this is that AIPAC is going to bring their immense power to bear against me.
**_[I’m one of the only members of Congress that doesn’t take money from AIPAC and who never has](https://link.email.greene4congress.)_.**
I don’t think it’s right. The truth is AIPAC needs to register as a foreign lobbyist by U.S. law because they are representing the __secular government__ of nuclear armed Israel 100%!!!
Look at what just happened, as soon as Congress was let out on recess and supposed to be working in our districts, AIPAC just took a bunch of members of Congress to Israel to make sure they don’t step out of line.
**While AIPAC is lying to their donors about me claiming I’m “betraying my American values” the REALITY is AIPAC serves and lobbies for a foreign country, Israel!!**
I’m as AMERICAN as they come!
**_[I can’t be bought and I’m not backing down](https://link.email.greene4congress.)_!!**
I’m fighting for my children’s generation and for AMERICA ONLY!!!!
_If AIPAC has a problem with that, I have one message for them:_
---
**BRING. IT. ON.**
I’ve always had the support of the conservative grassroots in my fight to force Washington to put America First, and now I’m up against the most powerful lobby in the history of our country.
**_[If you’ve been waiting to donate until something really important happened, now is the time to get back into the fight](https://link.email.greene4congress.)_.**
I will need it when they unleash their warchest in my primary.
For AMERICA,
| | [](https://link.email.greene4congress.)
---
**Marjorie Taylor Greene**
[](https://link.email.greene4congress.)

### **_[Unsubscribe from list](https://link.email.greene4congress.com/click/e5105843/VaHR0cHM6Ly9tdGcubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfZ3JlZW5lRk9VUmNvbmdyZXNzX2NvbTI1MDgwOA/LbmNsTUt5aVB2/qP2hpZHM9bmN5ZXd1N2RpTFlxYWxz/gaJZpgA/scp83c32bc1)_**

subject: Do the courts have the final say on the U.S. Constitution?
body:
CNN claims, “There are warnings that the U.S. is dangerously close to a constitutional crisis.”

| _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/u/va_1zkP5w_tEN74S_m2dqmX74LPWdJw9zEnK8NNYOIvO_YerJAjC14SsXQZg8ZU6-INv5AO7X6XM8FLVhDJSbLRPVH90SCMSW8kg3ZRMnTCZu0-kMYJEb65kpd_2syhv9d8FGl5NwGPUxh-VfmQmaF20WhTe4Lp75wp1aa7hrrvnmTqh0MCap_qM/pbWhY2RdeZjsAcPcz2DwaX8FLRAJD7Eu/11)_

[](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/mqze1alDnjm3VUV3J2nYmQeWohzPfx_1EPy3mse0ChGejHjpmAOewienYdhlTHBvqW0NBUPjsgjHJn1_yfZ99jhV8p4AQW0uoK1aufjL5jeek30oXZ1sBpnE1Ulu6scQZpERoesBxxF8qbB4Mo22qKXAGDH6_DcUlIQR90Q3-RD2GnkP4HZwHPcZj3pPx7RyJh5NmkzPVWFws9QKYYaVCjd9uwMCICesrk-QtKo7bTbDSBNau44VQwtMUIr3kKO6WCs-lCNM31ttWMieVzDF_H-sa9YGdm664GalK7vG31P98qdHenCxafZvPceS1639nwaO1dFU7X0IHmrrYAZjTA_EqxrZS1x1Mi_Kq7Jqx-XH3o6996-I83R5RsUW7FOnmWtWAO_vefAm9hgwETA7FT7oxWHlOfJ9dUJ7J3CFgngYIPnsNXNCWGXwRw7O1fckdVfi58kxVYzh_P4k89pMfwjmK2Yj2ypH17NGe4RYf7twjgKXjtYjVmVOr-0CYzNr0_OH7dxy2eM_TEWb2kK7iXzxaUfcJchNjwNoWpi9O19E0vNuY-nE3ngd0OFDft33VbI1GmeQngQg3jzYn8YxoIXCOsWo-jL_MdCvfOtGYiR3W8xWNLfjTP0TbzkPdP9Jb58yxDUXQW4PS4wJhpNNwPerEqAHdn-Hrd1MF3NtXKdxMe4EB1gKzOwEiXPFxdoyrQP-hn1K3_b4If1HWCM_CV8JBA9_xNpyYwhNdw8SYS-YYWRP3iv4yqQCwBclgtcYw7ShzrriBVoyPOE/Mjjm37td5LQgmqF8SMJjLhbglo0-BmSP/11) Dear Friend, CNN claims, "There are warnings that the U.S. is dangerously close to a constitutional crisis."

There is a constitutional crisis, but it's not what CNN thinks it is.

**The far-Left wants the current administration to obey the dictates of federal district court judges rather than the will of a majority of the American people.**

But do federal judges control the executive branch? Does the judicial branch have the final say in determining what is or is not constitutional?

Because the far-Left has undermined informed patriotism by promoting a distorted view of our nation's history and Constitution--including the judicial branch--many Americans do not know the answers to these questions.

That's why your participation in Hillsdale College's [**National Survey on the U.S. Judicial Branch**](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/ZMmkTOsGcUBlDyjP8JazFc8TSaABABYdPSwrykFXVt0j0nAXun4_uj9w68jYW5EfhjNmTwLMYwrBIQYTRZgZWiEsZGqPBHn7x3pFxixjMJRY7YNvmdExw-amEj_xCZ12t1tvx6RQbeYdKh2fkvyoeFHswQnvQPpRbik3-_r6VIdrOt7uAGlWdVC3nfrvcIaE3273iGQxAq1qCFrHuh2rKL24omj8osjHadljDvKPkxw-K_zD_LsSBkDDmtH5N9wc0g4BLqE4_uNjrW8VbdhtP3riBWEHE5bDB_AuwprwMrA70FID5wejPzYUQzL6dyXdzbT-itxsNjYZlzxGnFN910WI3Yv8YenS0m8aJ1YmG0Tu5NJ2MhSA17fFH_0EykKK7a0SNyCclWH4tNg3uFkhlLITfPpfNFv37X_2lpUWwVETkbAdGf-AVGG5JMyP0L5LgBUrH6oCwvzPZk6k7Z-dlE0sR3Oa9VBcVVqsyBoeFrITOB4JwNbXmki8pI19eHfqwlwMtjp7J3XKGkTVnGAbGdGu4NxyHIx5TvvN7divOp1mGWKQv5wsHPxUmEUZ0C5S0N8PiPD7-dp-88SF8yxhvM810r5MBXPcyAalbwdn-P8Tcs3Zmojtw0R4rMzFhMl6cY982l6NZhOPWL-eXHEN3PgiV7d4H6GPRaS98yMTyW5pBjJYq6ogWvLrHrpvP2fUwlGHn3XqnxCVJikSxypArqYbdq_lWnZZDOLabTg7HJ-RuA427R50oyRKSiTN946YPICVSJhivR6vAUY/vDl5obVhFtAidePih2c_t7uXmIEi8TQx/11) is urgently requested.

Your response will only take a few minutes, and the survey topic is crucial to the future of liberty and separation of powers in America.

You will help Hillsdale College form effective strategies to educate millions of Americans about the separation of powers and checks and balances--including the judiciary's proper role in our government.

You can easily complete your **National Survey on the U.S. Judicial Branch** using this secure link:

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/national-survey-on-the-us-judicial-branch**](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/bZ4XcjE8jAHN1ZlEzLfTvI8jKgPi-RiCf7iLtSyc-3mlBzLhUYt4wgA4AYFw-ukBJdrAlSsvOe0nQUI3mUqjU9YpfrmqahQ0SdtrtpIpaUm3pJrIlsOI0qX-iX8Y9uaHe4t8qK0tG6VCyMpppg9eTHJjgMi3fbgSYxbCOjPkQC76Yh8hOq3HKcVi_LDrWYwLH39Jm8MlMsuptIk0A5sRlbiXbPYxSRbPL0385hzqy8UE13xuHGOctIZDBriqQXgc0k9LWwL0ZMRgWuGoRXOPvmXcmgGYJ2HsDwW2GqDVlY9QCcb0F7X3DZXEbHFuVnYOxeIuSl60oSUmhbXHPQAixHoQTL1_qzHNeKZuYgTjh91rE63QG6Hu-ac36vEwXPmg1PijlKMU4LvM0ED9MZyK9i-9x5M8JPP37Du4sFDXZiMG9-2y6XIBR2N9MRl6t4FBtGr35Zb1TPIAqRYfRN-lU4rz7NX26sz2vDYqxRsn9-DlMlI5jrze8k_Uph3Bm3WjKGYW3uwmx5JficZ2a8iX-kZ55i6aUM2MJcfZmlpvd_MfLLWV2xfNeNZlmjiOy8nfXcGAH5S00b_KWur-YDudI7W22VghgOXsERGaB5XkVHMRemWRQ0-6dL8ssj8No_TwSGGXKVY36Q3wwn83DFhmPz6MO5h5bHdD6YIAZ2-lVa5G_FVPdbAjNSGHzN257eHoK5oJ4kuxaRa0BtQcGPz_NW15aAf5rpdvcnZuTwVhiFC10C3YCPFpRhB9KDSm7nUDg6JhHvXG7vWwGZQ/ZV8bNZJHn1_MleylF6e_OqGDj8q1nWZW/11)

There is a constitutional crisis, but it's not what CNN thinks it is. The far-Left wants the current administration to obey the dictates of federal district court judges rather than the will of a majority of the American people. But do federal judges control the executive branch? Does the judicial branch have the final say in determining what is or is not constitutional? Because the far-Left has undermined informed patriotism by promoting a distorted view of our nation's history and Constitution--including the judicial branch--many Americans do not know the answers to these questions. That's why your participation in Hillsdale College's is urgently requested. Your response will only take a few minutes, and the survey topic is crucial to the future of liberty and separation of powers in America. You will help Hillsdale College form effective strategies to educate millions of Americans about the separation of powers and checks and balances--including the judiciary's proper role in our government. name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: The story I hear time and time again
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
Seven months of the new Trump administration has sharply exposed the cracks in our economic foundation

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2612%2E4032634%2EgZxnDt)
---
This is a longer note — but it’s important. Plain and simple: Trump’s economy is not looking good. Inflation is up. Prices are soaring with new, outrageous tariffs taking effect recently. Job numbers are down. That’s the main story that Trump is desperately trying to run from right now — throwing every distraction at the wall from hanging out on the White House roof to firing the experts who objectively report on economic conditions. **But there’s a deeper story about the economy happening beyond the countless headlines and topline numbers that I want to talk about.** It’s a story that I hear from Americans time and time again: It’s harder and harder to get ahead, and it feels like the economy is rigged. We’re not imagining things. In fact, we have the data to back it up. It’s a little wonky, but when you dive in, there’s a clear picture that emerges — one where 40 years of policies aimed at enriching a handful at the top have opened cracks in our once-solid American middle class. So let’s rewind for a second — back to the year 2000, when *NSYNC topped the charts and America accounted for a quarter of all manufacturing in the entire world. Today, it’s 16%. Critical industries and supply chains have disappeared. From semiconductors to prescription drugs, America has outsourced even our most basic needs to other countries. What happened to the American worker in this story? Well, GDP kept right on climbing, but wages flattened out. The lowest-paid workers saw their wages rise just 6.5% in 40 years. But the top earners? Their incomes jumped by more than 40%. Meanwhile, costs for families exploded. Sure, flat-screen TVs were cheaper. But since 1990, the cost of housing is up 174%. Child care? A 220% increase. And health care is up a whopping 270%. From the outside looking in, it might look like the American economy is booming — the stock market hit record highs, inflation has receded from its peak, and unemployment is low. And there are plenty of headlines about “resilient” consumer spending. **But this obscures the troubling reality underneath: The wealthiest 10% of folks now account for _nearly half of all personal spending_. That’s up from 43% from just before the pandemic.** **So yes — Jeff Bezos may be spending $50 million on his Venetian wedding, but the majority of our country is struggling to afford simple pleasures like groceries and rent.** [**I’ve dedicated my career to fighting for policies that will make our economy work for the middle class — not just CEOs. If you’re with me in the fight for affordable housing, universal child care, paid family leave, and lowering the costs of healthcare — chip in what you can to power our grassroots-funded movement.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3085?t=8&akid=2612%2E4032634%2EgZxnDt)

---
_If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically:_ |
---
| [DONATE NOW: $3](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3085?t=22&express_lane=true&amount=3&akid=2612%2E4032634%2EgZxnDt)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $15](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3085?t=24&express_lane=true&amount=15&akid=2612%2E4032634%2EgZxnDt)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $28](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3085?t=26&express_lane=true&amount=28&akid=2612%2E4032634%2EgZxnDt)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $50](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3085?t=28&express_lane=true&amount=50&akid=2612%2E4032634%2EgZxnDt)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $100](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3085?t=18&express_lane=true&amount=100&akid=2612%2E4032634%2EgZxnDt)
---
| [OTHER AMOUNT](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3085?t=20&express_lane=true&akid=2612%2E4032634%2EgZxnDt)
---

---
Seven months of the new Trump administration has sharply exposed those cracks in our economic foundation and strained an already-precarious situation. Trump and the GOP wasted no time widening that divide between the top 10% and everyone else by:
* Kicking 17 million people off their health insurance to give handouts to billionaires;
* Stripping food assistance from families to give Meta and Google another tax cut;
* Playing games with arbitrary, chaotic tariffs that are raising the cost of everything from a cup of coffee to prescription drugs.

From a president who promised to lower costs on “Day One”, it’s been a one-two-three punch of tariff chaos, deficit-busting tax giveaways, and financial deregulation that can eventually blow up this economy. And, once again, the pain will fall mostly on America’s working families. **But there’s another way forward. We’ve got to do more than just contain the damage from Trump. That’s why I’m leading the fight for investments in a new economy that will actually work for working people. Here are a few places to start:**
* We’ve got to build more housing, cut red tape, and take on corporate landlords. My new bipartisan bill would build three million new or rehabilitated homes, lower rents by 10%, and save the average family $140 per month.
* Second, we’ve got to support child care. The average yearly cost for child care in DC is $25,480. That’s outrageous. Investing in affordable, high-quality child care would let us lower costs for families and grow our labor force.
* Finally, we’ve got to make investments in education and health care, giving people a chance to build better skills and stay healthier.

The problem is pretty straightforward. Too many economic policies in Washington are short-sighted, shaped by the rich and powerful, to benefit the rich and powerful. But the American people have had enough. They may shift around on who they think will deliver change, but the demand for change is growing stronger. All we need is the will to relentlessly put working people first — building an economy that all Americans can finally afford. That’s what I’m calling on the Democratic party to lead on. [**I’m going to continue fighting my heart out for this. We’ve got the vision. We just need more leaders fighting for it. If you’re with me, please chip in $28 or anything you can so we can continue campaigning for a government that works for working people.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3085?amount=28&t=14&akid=2612%2E4032634%2EgZxnDt) Thanks for reading — and thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth
| | | |
---
| [DONATE](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3085?t=12&akid=2612%2E4032634%2EgZxnDt)
---

name: Morgan Stahr, United for Democracy
subject: They’re going to rip apart the Voting Rights Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by United for Democracy
body:
Texas is trying to rig the game. | |
---
Peter, **Texas Republicans recently unveiled their redistricting plan.** Their proposed congressional map would create a solid-red MAGA five-seat advantage: [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38jvf5GaclcQ7i_R9B-DMhJQTb2iZqQrPDa-yX8kVBgp-J9HGzeRXaOz3WhLM6bWnmc8hlLQk2nG5-RCYFnupe96rIwWPM2b2nmaDMwRP9o8lXktr2S9cpTyq87L_oXbshf13nWJCaMXxTTwn5GnP2domJKF4s7L01tiXh6c3rZIpBtAXzMACKuaUnd__kUQHmEJ32SqyVUi8NeazrOEUk75CD58zVMPnhuN-NsGiDPib87Q55LEQQVepLuj7Jn0xa0zmUfJCziL7xWjZyib3DNXeG_qCB11jpdwAt0KshNyeg08vvc9516Np5Gntks4XKb1KDl_ypv2yWC9OuzAJJ4PPdTgB3sGEzPKlO_4F4ZITPrNLAZAlD3L77xDd-_QMI/4iv/FDuR4hJKQGOLOu4Ev-1qlw/h0/h001.CfMOUClbTcPAcuG0lWMUjuvZh2ujzIfD2Kjo5zf5w7E) This is what manipulating democracy looks like. **The Texas Republican map would specifically shift Democrat-held seats to Republicans—allowing them to pick up five more seats in Congress.** The illegally drawn map came at the prompting of Trump’s own Department of Justice, who claimed that districts containing a majority Black and Hispanic population were unfair. The new map, as a result, is racially targeted toward highly-populated, majority-minority areas—with a specific aim to dilute the voting power of these demographics. **It’s a disaster for the Voting Rights Act.** This case is likely to go all the way to the Supreme Court. And we know better than to rely on Trump’s MAGA justices to protect our rights and our democracy. [**Congress has the power to step in and ensure our voting districts are drawn fairly. Add your name now and tell Congress to stop gerrymandering and pass national redistricting reform now.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38jvf5GaclcQ7i_R9B-DMhJQTb2iZqQrPDa-yX8kVBgp-J9HGzeRXaOz3WhLM6bWnmc8hlLQk2nG5-RCYFnupe96rIwWPM2b2nmaDMwRP9o8nhsTYouOgn_GYZXdoF0uSxmZ5OodTKi5qIfUUN89sBWwI4mn7yPWai83IxThmJSCqS-UiyeawiEoJqgEZKotjyYes-GMWMqC5l9XwPb4HWx7wc14daoq6KpEfe5VX8p9K68qyMuZ9xMasYwHLRunQBpB37R8LImE22pNkE56ApX7IaNEqmeFoIdKSb23MqBE5Vu6nJjh0lTpNc2onR29F0QI2SMp9go4jBi6s5aCZSw9wYjtUC2nq60pSwcUKO2XmfcewQF3co2rII3QeRXkaA/4iv/FDuR4hJKQGOLOu4Ev-1qlw/h1/h001.rjs27alAPR-kkJzB_yUjwjQfHwcIonKEDKKkqma_42Y) [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38jvf5GaclcQ7i_R9B-DMhJQTb2iZqQrPDa-yX8kVBgp-J9HGzeRXaOz3WhLM6bWnmc8hlLQk2nG5-RCYFnupe96rIwWPM2b2nmaDMwRP9o8gmbJ2IJOATIA2E-g-CMcOS1LIgRU9rz44qEzN-Vs5JAWhDezkkvQSqZUmfZq9FxzekZgbOIJbw_qEQWHjTvhan7IfTw4M1cUYHb_njdRXqaMN0QGeAz-M4aJ1uamNzvmbSt2cBNIDPHZ2QNO3QLOaX8b7sDknEDjXZURVWRWE4dfVCXCAKEb8J8VWLhy-MzyeSPT4NJo0803FWmnBifx9Z0afDaZSlEX_ifdWf5ZviD72LHS2TZqkj2NedI4wZdUJGMtFi5eoI7i_t03rkP2X4/4iv/FDuR4hJKQGOLOu4Ev-1qlw/h2/h001.20QnugQgTrDhTvsu1oftnkv-8WEAuEvKIlkySfz3BQc) It’s very likely that the Supreme Court will issue a decision on gerrymandering in Louisiana soon—which would have clear implications for congressional maps across the country. **Experts suspect that the MAGA justices on the Court may announce a ruling that essentially rips apart the Voting Rights Act—the very law that would protect Texas’ citizens if their new congressional map came before the Court.** It’s clear that Donald Trump, Republican state legislators, and even the Supreme Court is willing to trample our Constitution and our rights in order to consolidate more authoritarian power. **Congress must act before it’s too late.** [**Demand your representative and senators stop illegal gerrymandering. clean_body: Texas is trying to rig the game. | | Peter, Texas Republicans recently unveiled their redistricting plan. Their proposed congressional map would create a solid-red MAGA five-seat advantage: This is what manipulating democracy looks like. The Texas Republican map would specifically shift Democrat-held seats to Republicans—allowing them to pick up five more seats in Congress. The illegally drawn map came at the prompting of Trump’s own Department of Justice, who claimed that districts containing a majority Black and Hispanic population were unfair. The new map, as a result, is racially targeted toward highly-populated, majority-minority areas—with a specific aim to dilute the voting power of these demographics. It’s a disaster for the Voting Rights Act. This case is likely to go all the way to the Supreme Court. And we know better than to rely on Trump’s MAGA justices to protect our rights and our democracy. It’s very likely that the Supreme Court will issue a decision on gerrymandering in Louisiana soon—which would have clear implications for congressional maps across the country. Experts suspect that the MAGA justices on the Court may announce a ruling that essentially rips apart the Voting Rights Act—the very law that would protect Texas’ citizens if their new congressional map came before the Court. It’s clear that Donald Trump, Republican state legislators, and even the Supreme Court is willing to trample our Constitution and our rights in order to consolidate more authoritarian power. Congress must act before it’s too late. Thank you for fighting gerrymandering, Morgan Stahr United for Democracy Paid for by United for Democracy. | | Sent via . clean_body: | | Peter, Since stepping into office, he’s voted to slash Medicaid and SNAP, two lifelines for millions of Pennsylvanians. He’s rubber-stamped policies that actively harm working families. And he’s done it all while pocketing up to $6 million in shady stock trades. We’re talking about a guy who looked at Congress and saw an opportunity... not to serve, but to self-enrich. You know the type, cuts for the poor, perks for himself, and the title of “public servant” as the bow on top. So who are we talking about? Take a wild guess(if you’ve been watching the headlines, you probably already know). It's Rob Bresnahan. Of course it is. If that name alone makes your blood boil like it does ours, we need you with us, right now. We need your help to hold Rob Bresnahan accountable and flip this seat. = | | = This is a pattern. While every day Pennsylvanians work multiple jobs just to afford groceries, Bresnahan is busy voting to cut SNAP. While families rely on Medicaid to keep their children healthy, Bresnahan votes to gut it. Then he turns around and celebrates a tax bill that lines his pockets. He doesn’t work for you. He never did. And the longer he stays in office, the more damage he’ll do. If you’re tired of seeing politicians like Bresnahan rig the system against us, = | = — PA Dems = | Follow us on , , and **! name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
subject: Rent-free
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
Last week, we updated you on Trump sending at least 4 emails attacking Alexandria, fundraising off of her calls for his impeachment. **It hasn't stopped** :
[](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250808.fwWA_R&refcode=em20250808-9507-P_FND_EGR_CTR&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9507%2E2526796%2EwCmCkZ)
Trump is raking in donations from corporations, billionaires, and special interests. He’s working around the clock to turn this country into a slush fund for his donor pals while the rest of us fight over scraps.
But **Alexandria is living in Trump's head rent-free because she _won’t_ back down from standing up for what is right,** regardless of the insults and threats directed at her by billionaires or Donald Trump.
This might have him rattled, too:
[](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250808.fwWA_R&refcode=em20250808-9507-P_FND_EGR_CTR&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9507%2E2526796%2EwCmCkZ)
But Alexandria can't do this work alone. She needs a movement alongside her.
Every supporter who chips in from NY-14 and beyond shows the strength for our progressive movement and will help us fight for social, racial, environmental, and economic justice.
[Will you make a $5 contributino today to show your support for Alexandria and her calls for Trump’s impeachment? Every donation makes a difference in our grassroots fight to demand better for the working people of this country. ](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250808.fwWA_R&refcode=em20250808-9507-P_FND_EGR_CTR&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9507%2E2526796%2EwCmCkZ)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250808.fwWA_R&refcode=em20250808-9507-P_FND_EGR_CTR&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=9507%2E2526796%2EwCmCkZ)**
Whether you can donate today or not, thank you for standing with us.
In solidarity,
Team AOC

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250808?refcode=em20250808-9507-P_FND_EGR_CTR&t=23&refcode2=9507_2526796_wCmCkZ&akid=9507%2E2526796%2EwCmCkZ)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250808-9507-P_FND_EGR_CTR&t=24&akid=9507%2E2526796%2EwCmCkZ)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=25&akid=9507%2E2526796%2EwCmCkZ) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=26&akid=9507%2E2526796%2EwCmCkZ) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=27&akid=9507%2E2526796%2EwCmCkZ) | [](https://bsky.app/profile/aoc.bsky.social?t=28&akid=9507%2E2526796%2EwCmCkZ)
---|---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

I’ve been around Montana politics for awhile now, and here’s what I know: **Listening builds trust. Listening will deliver results._Listening is how we lead._**

**Reilly Neill is on the ground listening to Montanans, working with Montanans, and fighting for Montanans. That’s why she has my full support.**

I’m proud to endorse her for the U.S. Senate because I trust her to stay in the saddle no matter how hard it bucks. We need fighters like Reilly Neill.

**Reilly’s taking that fight to the U.S. Senate, and she needs us riding with her.**

_This is the 3rd part of a 5 Part Series on Dismantling the Democrat Atom Bomb about the Big Beautiful Bill and its "devastating" impact on healthcare. _
_For Part 1-**The Medicaid Paradox - Growth, Costs, and Misleading "Cuts"** \- [Click Here](https://substack.com/redirect/61a6d742-db1a-49e5-87b6-fc4ed273f165?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
For Part 2: _**The Unjust Burden - How the Current System Fails Working Americans **[Click Here](https://substack.com/redirect/3c7df5c8-9563-4a3f-85ab-2114e463987c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/84149b75-63de-4e48-8620-a355a8fe877e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

Photo by [Stock Birken](https://substack.com/redirect/8799d9ef-3d94-44b2-b998-97ace364467a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on [Unsplash](https://substack.com/redirect/5ae7f1c1-4f8a-42b1-878e-0fb28adf61b9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
## Part 3: The Monopoly Problem - How Consolidation Drives Up Healthcare Costs
Over the past two decades, U.S. healthcare costs have nearly doubled (inflation adjusted), exceeding $5 trillion annually. A major driver of this exponential growth is the widespread consolidation within the healthcare industry, leading to anti-competitive practices and inflated prices that burden consumers, employers, and individuals.
[Pledge your support](https://substack..zDWfmQ6deZ3MkZWAiCfkc5GEVZHhLs34FeAlx2MYi5A?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=170176846)
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=170176846&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwMTc2ODQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTQ2ODk2NDYsImV4cCI6MTc1NzI4MTY0NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.VocS5tWFwGBAwzvjAgcNlZpmVp-ocgQiOIPHfDg1TTI)
**The Rise of Mega Hospital Systems:** Through hospital mergers and acquisitions of private practices, often through VC roll-ups or by hospital networks, the healthcare landscape has become dominated by a few powerful, vertically-integrated hospital systems.
**Lack of Bargaining Power Results in the Roll-up of Private Practices:** The large difference in reimbursement rates between hospital-affiliated providers and independent private practices is _**extensive**_. Hospitals secure much higher payments for the same services. While many independent private practices--often more cost-effective providers (that have a knowledge and familiarity with a patient and their family), they cannot compete. As a result, they are either 1) driven out of business or 2) pressured into acquisition by larger systems (which are usually part of a hospital network or feed a hospital network). This is at the same time, their employed physicians (in the practices that they acquired) feed extensive downstream revenue that the physician does not benefit from.
Consolidation reduces competition and to the victor goes the bargaining power that does not exist in private practice today. **As early as 2010, the Attorney General of Massachusetts found that healthcare costs could vary by 300% based on the bargaining power of the healthcare system**. This imbalance allows consolidated systems to demand significantly higher reimbursements from insurance companies, which are then passed on to consumers in the form of higher premiums.
_**In Connecticut, the Hospital Networks of Yale and Hartford Health are some of the largest private employers in the state.**_
_**This trend further reduces competition and consumer choice, and ensures that more expensive and care models (an their excessive administrative burdens) will prevail.**_
**Consolidation at All Levels - Insurance Companies:** Consolidation isn't limited to just providers; insurance companies have also undergone significant consolidation, leading to markets often dominated by a single insurer in the name of "efficiency".
In theory, this was supposed to result in more leverage and lower prices between insurance company and providers (at a cost to providers) resulting in lower premiums. In turn, these "savings" were supposed to be passed on to consumers in the form of lower premiums.
The result of this reduced competition in the insurance market also contributes to higher costs for policyholders, lower reimbursements to providers and the winners are shareholders and CEOs as denial rates for claims increase costing you the policyholder even more for your healthcare. It also results in offsetting lower provider fees to hospitals by placing even more fee compression to independent practitioners.
**Outdated Antitrust Laws:** Ironically, government policies and the enforcement of outdated antitrust laws have inadvertently contributed to this consolidation. These laws of the 1800s and early 1900s hinder physicians and other independent providers from forming collaborations to negotiate fairer reimbursements and reduce administrative burdens from large insurance companies. This reality is that Doctors are unable to form a union except employed physicians (like those who work in hospitals) so this never promotes better negotiating power for physicians.
This inability to collectively bargain disincentivizes smaller, more agile providers. This lack of protection has fully paved the way for the dominance of larger, more expensive entities.
The result is a healthcare system where competition is stifled, and costs continue to climb unchecked with no guardrails and no ability to change the provider model. It also results in every doctor you go to being part of a vertically integrated hospital model (even when you go to an off-site office).
Problems arise when patients can't get appointments for semi-urgent medical issues and are forced to go to Urgent Care or Emergency Departments which are both run by hospital networks. The result is care that feels like taking a number at the deli counter coupled with endless music on hold for appointments.
**Stay Tuned for -** Part 4: The Human Cost - Physician Burnout and Dissatisfaction

name: Ron Wyden
subject: Republicans are so eager to cut Social Security they’ve given the game away
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Wyden for Senate
body:
Did you see what Trump’s Treasury Secretary said?
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112991343/562283203/2140832878?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZTBjOWQ4My1hMTc0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Iox1wHZBELEmBtanSq7Pp_qYZyj1zPzO2MWvZljthTo=&emci=1f580554-9774-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=5e0c9d83-a174-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=9818364)
---

| | I've sounded the alarm for months about how giddy Republicans are to cut Social Security. It turns out Trump's Treasury Secretary was _so_ excited that last week, he publicly bragged that the newly-passed GOP budget bill contained – his words, not mine – **"****a backdoor for privatizing Social Security."** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112991344/562283204/-1155685579?ms=2025-08-08-social-security&refcode=2025-08-08-social-security&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZTBjOWQ4My1hMTc0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Iox1wHZBELEmBtanSq7Pp_qYZyj1zPzO2MWvZljthTo=&emci=1f580554-9774-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=5e0c9d83-a174-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=9818364) We need to send a message to Trump, his cronies, and the GOP: **if you come for Social Security, you're going to have to get through us.** Let's show them that any effort to cut or privatize Social Security – _especially_ something snuck into a budget bill under the guise of "investment accounts" – is going to be met with powerful opposition. clean_body: Did you see what Trump’s Treasury Secretary said? | | | | I've sounded the alarm for months about how giddy Republicans are to cut Social Security. It turns out Trump's Treasury Secretary was so excited that last week, he publicly bragged that the newly-passed GOP budget bill contained – his words, not mine – "a backdoor for privatizing Social Security." We need to send a message to Trump, his cronies, and the GOP: if you come for Social Security, you're going to have to get through us. Let's show them that any effort to cut or privatize Social Security – especially something snuck into a budget bill under the guise of "investment accounts" – is going to be met with powerful opposition. The last time Republicans made a real push to privatize Social Security was in 2005. Democrats found our voice and regained our political footing by standing up for the right of Americans to retire with dignity. It was the start of a comeback that led to us retaking Congress and the White House. We need to adopt the same approach in 2025, and amplifying our voices is the first step. ## **The Soundcheck Fund: A Mic for the Next Generation of Political Influencers**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/42598932-d396-43b4-930d-d5e7e8dda763?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**What if the next superstar 's trapped in anonymity by right-wing algorithms? Burning out at a 9-to-5 without the time to use skills to save our democracy? Worse, if institutions, donors, and those with big platforms refused to help, so we lost this generational talent to corporate PR...After all, we all must feed our families.**
_**What if you could be the one to help them break through?**_
Welcome to the **Soundcheck Fund** --a Blue Amp initiative designed to discover, amplify, and support emerging voices. Think of it as a launchpad for political messaging--_scrappy, independent, and built to amplify what corporate media ignores._
* * *
### **🎧 Why We Built This**
The loudest voices in politics aren't always the smartest--just the best-funded.
Sadly, it's often not talent that wins attention anymore. It's ad spends. Insider access. Algorithms attuned to outrage.
The result?
* Authentic voices get drowned out.
* Smart commentary is hidden.
* New leaders never make it past the loading screen.

**We 're here to change that.**
* * *
### **The Premise Is Simple**
We're seeding a new generation of creators--those who speak with clarity, urgency, humor, and fire. Those who'd go viral _if they had time, funding, and support._
With the Soundcheck Fund, we give talented independent media purveyors:
* A platform.
* A payment.
* A push.

You don't need to be polished--it's often better if you're not. You don't need perfect lighting or expensive gear. You just need something _real_ to say, and the guts to say it.
* * *
### **Who Should Apply?**
If this hits home, we're probably talking to you:
* You've been ranting to your friends about politics for years.
* You've got a take that _makes sense_ --and nobody says it quite the way you do.
* You've got an audience, or the bones of one, but no time/money to build it out.
* You've never been invited to the table--but you've been building your own.

We'll help you turn that into a _micro-platform_ --fast.
You'll publish your own content. We'll feature your work. You'll get a micro-grant. And we'll send readers and allies your way.
Apply [HERE](https://substack.com/redirect/b16bac6f-bcb3-4b62-b303-421cc0c3d118?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)!
* * *
### **💡 Who Should Fund This?**
If you've ever said:
> > _" Why don't we have our own Joe Rogan, Theo Von, or Ben Shapiro?"_
>> _" Why are we always playing catch-up in the messaging war?"_
>> _" Where are the new voices who actually get it?"_
**... then this fund--[which you can support with a paid Blue Amp subscription](https://substack.com/redirect/0f7622eb-55d5-4520-a539-f9bfae6674a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--is your answer.**
Supporters of the Soundcheck Fund aren't just donors--they're _talent scouts for a more democratic and better future_. The ones who can say:
> > _" Yeah, I backed them before anyone knew their name."_
You don't need to bankroll a media empire. [You just need to help one person speak up](https://substack.com/redirect/0f7622eb-55d5-4520-a539-f9bfae6674a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
### **📣 First Cohort: Already Making Noise**
We've already picked the first few recipients and given them their awards, and we'll announce their names nexr week! They've been doing exactly what we hoped:
* Calling out corporate candidates in plain language.
* Writing essays that feel like a slap in the face (in a good way).
* Podcasting with the urgency of people who know this moment matters.

They're not polished. **They 're **_**real**_**.** And that's exactly what this moment demands.
* * *
### **🫵 What You Can Do Right Now**
* **Apply** to join the next cohort -> [Right Here](https://substack.com/redirect/b16bac6f-bcb3-4b62-b303-421cc0c3d118?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Donate** to fund the next voice -> [Right Here](https://substack.com/redirect/0f7622eb-55d5-4520-a539-f9bfae6674a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Forward** this to someone who _needs_ to hear it.

* * *
The best voices in politics aren't waiting for permission. They're waiting for a _mic_.
This is that mic.
Let's turn it on.
* * *
## The Most MAGA Superman's The Worst Human. Who Could've Guessed?
Dean Cain--who portrayed Superman on a tv show that lasted like three years and began its run before the existence of the Smartphone--took to his Instagram this past week.
No, it wasn't for one of his usual gigs, like pushing faith-based gold--he peddles the Genesis Gold Group, not kidding--by scaring people that "China is targeting your bank account." Or even reverse mortgages.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3fdae469-f85b-4a57-b144-e0d02e4bb3be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
`Folks, The Number of New Paid Subscribers has Dramatically Slowed this Past Week. Without Them, We Can't Continue to Expand The Soundcheck Fund--Just as Trump Kills the Corporation for Public Broadcasting. Please Consider a Paid Subscription to Support the Growth of Independent Media `
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.lTnKPP9_evMhpExdq-W9AJjOQMVeI855pEperpDMlpo?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=170448236)
The oft-unnoticed, Trump-humping "actor,"--whom I can only guess from his God Gold really needs the money -- [announced he joined ICE to "protect his country."](https://substack.com/redirect/a8ce0ab6-cdee-4763-be95-e7011e5bed14?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) _With John Williams ' famous Superman theme music blaring in the background_. Seriously.
First... _**dude**_. You haven't played Superman since Princess Diana was alive and Kylie Jenner wasn't. There really has to be a statute of limitations on these things, or should I add my stint as student body president in grad school back on my resume?
Second, before anyone even knew you were a right-wing clodpole, you were easily considered the sh*ttiest performer to ever don the cape. I mean, you made Kevin Sorbo's Hercules seem like Shakespeare. Hell George Reeves, who played Kal-El, could do a better Superman than you right now and he's been dead for 65 years.
Finally, yes, we know you need the $50,000 ICE bonus or you might have to add boner pills to your impressive God-Gold-pimping repertoire, but don't you think that would've been less humiliating? How about porn?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/20f83793-7e8f-451b-a789-59123cf79c21?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I mean, what is it that attracted you to ICE? Was it the [sickening violence against peaceful workers](https://substack.com/redirect/53748b17-f3b0-4e3e-be6d-b5258ba1b5d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)? The ripping families apart? The putting up a concentration camp in Florida [to make Trump donors huge windfalls of cash](https://substack.com/redirect/bf7eef85-519c-42d9-b35d-4a155b4b9d07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)? ICE agents throwing a teenage U.S. citizen to the ground and saying "[sorry, you have no rights here, amigo](https://substack.com/redirect/0b2fdb1f-e778-4180-a5a4-bba744380481?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)?" Ripping a 4th grader out of her class to deport her?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/52cab10a-2860-4721-9a3f-88ec2e75e880?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Truth, justice and the American way! Am I right!?_
So, ok, you want to join a racist Stasi [run by Lyin' Donald Trump](https://substack.com/redirect/8086763c-a219-4232-890a-4c455f8334ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). So I have a few questions I'd like to ask you about your--even for a Republican--incredible freakin hypocrisy.
Did you read enough of your scripts to know that Superman was an alien, you schmuck? Is the fact that this was a story about this alien coming to the heart of America, Kansas, and being taken in by regular folk and raised like their own...is that something that ever surfed along the river of cranial leakage your call a cerebrum?
Did you need to get attention that badly by going on Instagram and stealing the Superman music? Maybe there is a Genesis Platinum contract on the line?
Also, did you read your religious texts you claimed to revere when you went to the Wailing Wall and searched the streets of Israel "for the prophets?" Or when you moved away from Hollywood (totes not because nobody would hire you) "driven by your deepening faith in Jesus Christ?" And then Genesis Gold too! Huzzah!
We get it--you love Jesus!
But--and I don't mean [to steal from my friend and Amped Up guest, John Fuglesang](https://substack.com/redirect/4dd98c51-6314-44bc-9316-970e2c20aa1e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), here--do you have a clue what Jesus, ya know, said? You ever check out the "Parable of the Stranger?" Because I have some news for you--brace yourself--Jesus wouldn't have donned a mask, no identification and thrown brown people to the ground just for the psychopathy of it.
He talked about taking in the stranger, helping the traveler on the road who needed lodging, food, etc. Not throwing them through a car window and then sending them to El Salvador. Just, ya know, in case that part was lost in translation. Also, Jesus wasn't all into beating down on people--at all--so pulverizing an "amigo with no rights" wouldn't have been his kink.
And let's end this part of the Dean Cain is a dunderheaded arse show with one word: Tanaka. Yeah, your real name.
_You've talked in the past about how members of your family were put in Japanese internment during World War II._ Which is horrifying, and I'd offer my sympathies. But now this--and worse--is exactly what you're bragging about wanting to do on Instagram while playing iconic music for justice?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/067cfbee-e134-42f1-9eb3-ca7c717eab56?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
So simple question here, big guy, and, no, it's not about why Brenda ditched you for Dillon. Easy choice. He was a cool loner. You were a try-hard dork. But I'm sure you were just "acting," yes?
What I'm getting at is: how many ways can one unemployed actor be [a lie-spitting, Trump-snorting, ungodly, f*king hypocrite](https://substack.com/redirect/0e3a7bc1-e6d5-4289-a16c-f1e4d1ee7d30?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)? You betray the spirit and purpose of the biggest role you played. You betray the religion and God you claim to cherish. You betray your family's ethnic background and painful history.
Quite a threesome there, Dean. Or what your fellow Trump supporter, Kid Rock, would call "another night with the clap."
So, in any case, Dean, maybe go somewhere else, where your bigotry, sophistry and douchery are more appreciated. Steven Seagal and David Duke have lived in Russia and love Putin. I gotta believe, with your record, you'd love Pootie-Poot too. Just sayin.
Or just jump on a SpaceX and see if you can make it to Krypton. If you do, you can play the Superman music all you want!
To paraphrase Jack Nicholson from _As Good As It Gets_ (from 1997! The last season of Lois & Clark...and your career!), a man who's obviously had to toil in the immense shadow of your thespianism for years: sell your crazy somewhere else, [we're all stocked up here in America](https://substack.com/redirect/ac888ce8-61c2-4f90-a15b-a36ad0db9083?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
#### _**[THIS WEEK ON AMPED UP!](https://substack.com/redirect/f3d5b58f-07e0-42cc-811f-6de2d70703f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
We talked with great ad man Will Robinson about how Dems need a whole new strategy to connect with people. Authentic connection, between neighbors, colleagues, friends and influencers they've know via two-way convos on social media.
Democrats need to deliver messages from those whom voters trust (which, speaking as a former ad guy, has been obvious for a long time!). Also, [Dems must be much more aggressive](https://substack.com/redirect/2294c6a0-fbc7-4d22-942d-a53ea3607bfe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Much more in here, watch!
[](https://substack.com/redirect/6249d855-2b32-4def-8b72-96374718241b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

Please Consider a Paid Subscription to Support the Growth of Independent Media ` Cliff 's Note: My Column Comes Right After... The Soundcheck Fund: A Mic for the Next Generation of Political Influencers | | ---|---|--- What if the next superstar 's trapped in anonymity by right-wing algorithms? Burning out at a 9-to-5 without the time to use skills to save our democracy? Worse, if institutions, donors, and those with big platforms refused to help, so we lost this generational talent to corporate PR...After all, we all must feed our families. What if you could be the one to help them break through? Welcome to the Soundcheck Fund --a Blue Amp initiative designed to discover, amplify, and support emerging voices. Think of it as a launchpad for political messaging--scrappy, independent, and built to amplify what corporate media ignores. 🎧 Why We Built This The loudest voices in politics aren't always the smartest--just the best-funded. Sadly, it's often not talent that wins attention anymore. It's ad spends. Insider access. Algorithms attuned to outrage. The result? Authentic voices get drowned out. Smart commentary is hidden. New leaders never make it past the loading screen. We 're here to change that. The Premise Is Simple We're seeding a new generation of creators--those who speak with clarity, urgency, humor, and fire. Those who'd go viral if they had time, funding, and support. With the Soundcheck Fund, we give talented independent media purveyors: A platform. A payment. A push. You don't need to be polished--it's often better if you're not. You don't need perfect lighting or expensive gear. You just need something real to say, and the guts to say it. Who Should Apply? If this hits home, we're probably talking to you: You've been ranting to your friends about politics for years. You've got a take that makes sense --and nobody says it quite the way you do. You've got an audience, or the bones of one, but no time/money to build it out. You've never been invited to the table--but you've been building your own. We'll help you turn that into a micro-platform --fast. You'll publish your own content. We'll feature your work. You'll get a micro-grant. And we'll send readers and allies your way. Apply ! 💡 Who Should Fund This? If you've ever said: > " Why don't we have our own Joe Rogan, Theo Von, or Ben Shapiro?" > " Why are we always playing catch-up in the messaging war?" > " Where are the new voices who actually get it?" ... then this fund-- your answer. Supporters of the Soundcheck Fund aren't just donors--they're talent scouts for a more democratic and better future. The ones who can say: > " Yeah, I backed them before anyone knew their name." You don't need to bankroll a media empire. . 📣 First Cohort: Already Making Noise We've already picked the first few recipients and given them their awards, and we'll announce their names nexr week! They've been doing exactly what we hoped: Calling out corporate candidates in plain language. Writing essays that feel like a slap in the face (in a good way). Podcasting with the urgency of people who know this moment matters. They're not polished. They 're real. And that's exactly what this moment demands. 🫵 What You Can Do Right Now Apply to join the next cohort -> Donate to fund the next voice -> Forward this to someone who needs to hear it. The best voices in politics aren't waiting for permission. They're waiting for a mic. This is that mic. Let's turn it on. The Most MAGA Superman's The Worst Human. Who Could've Guessed? Dean Cain--who portrayed Superman on a tv show that lasted like three years and began its run before the existence of the Smartphone--took to his Instagram this past week. No, it wasn't for one of his usual gigs, like pushing faith-based gold--he peddles the Genesis Gold Group, not kidding--by scaring people that "China is targeting your bank account." Or even reverse mortgages. | | ---|---|--- `Folks, The Number of New Paid Subscribers has Dramatically Slowed this Past Week. Without Them, We Can't Continue to Expand The Soundcheck Fund--Just as Trump Kills the Corporation for Public Broadcasting. Please Consider a Paid Subscription to Support the Growth of Independent Media ` The oft-unnoticed, Trump-humping "actor,"--whom I can only guess from his God Gold really needs the money -- With John Williams ' famous Superman theme music blaring in the background. Seriously. First... dude. You haven't played Superman since Princess Diana was alive and Kylie Jenner wasn't. There really has to be a statute of limitations on these things, or should I add my stint as student body president in grad school back on my resume? Second, before anyone even knew you were a right-wing clodpole, you were easily considered the shttiest performer to ever don the cape. I mean, you made Kevin Sorbo's Hercules seem like Shakespeare. Hell George Reeves, who played Kal-El, could do a better Superman than you right now and he's been dead for 65 years. Finally, yes, we know you need the $50,000 ICE bonus or you might have to add boner pills to your impressive God-Gold-pimping repertoire, but don't you think that would've been less humiliating? How about porn? | | ---|---|--- I mean, what is it that attracted you to ICE? Was it the ? The ripping families apart? The putting up a concentration camp in Florida ? ICE agents throwing a teenage U.S. citizen to the ground and saying "?" Ripping a 4th grader out of her class to deport her? | | ---|---|--- Truth, justice and the American way! Am I right!? So, ok, you want to join a racist Stasi . So I have a few questions I'd like to ask you about your--even for a Republican--incredible freakin hypocrisy. Did you read enough of your scripts to know that Superman was an alien, you schmuck? Is the fact that this was a story about this alien coming to the heart of America, Kansas, and being taken in by regular folk and raised like their own...is that something that ever surfed along the river of cranial leakage your call a cerebrum? Did you need to get attention that badly by going on Instagram and stealing the Superman music? Maybe there is a Genesis Platinum contract on the line? Also, did you read your religious texts you claimed to revere when you went to the Wailing Wall and searched the streets of Israel "for the prophets?" Or when you moved away from Hollywood (totes not because nobody would hire you) "driven by your deepening faith in Jesus Christ?" And then Genesis Gold too! Huzzah! We get it--you love Jesus! But--and I don't mean , here--do you have a clue what Jesus, ya know, said? You ever check out the "Parable of the Stranger?" Because I have some news for you--brace yourself--Jesus wouldn't have donned a mask, no identification and thrown brown people to the ground just for the psychopathy of it. He talked about taking in the stranger, helping the traveler on the road who needed lodging, food, etc. Not throwing them through a car window and then sending them to El Salvador. Just, ya know, in case that part was lost in translation. Also, Jesus wasn't all into beating down on people--at all--so pulverizing an "amigo with no rights" wouldn't have been his kink. And let's end this part of the Dean Cain is a dunderheaded arse show with one word: Tanaka. Yeah, your real name. You've talked in the past about how members of your family were put in Japanese internment during World War II. Which is horrifying, and I'd offer my sympathies. But now this--and worse--is exactly what you're bragging about wanting to do on Instagram while playing iconic music for justice? | | ---|---|--- So simple question here, big guy, and, no, it's not about why Brenda ditched you for Dillon. Easy choice. He was a cool loner. You were a try-hard dork. But I'm sure you were just "acting," yes? What I'm getting at is: how many ways can one unemployed actor be ? You betray the spirit and purpose of the biggest role you played. You betray the religion and God you claim to cherish. You betray your family's ethnic background and painful history. Quite a threesome there, Dean. Or what your fellow Trump supporter, Kid Rock, would call "another night with the clap." So, in any case, Dean, maybe go somewhere else, where your bigotry, sophistry and douchery are more appreciated. Steven Seagal and David Duke have lived in Russia and love Putin. I gotta believe, with your record, you'd love Pootie-Poot too. Just sayin. Or just jump on a SpaceX and see if you can make it to Krypton. If you do, you can play the Superman music all you want! To paraphrase Jack Nicholson from As Good As It Gets (from 1997! The last season of Lois & Clark...and your career!), a man who's obviously had to toil in the immense shadow of your thespianism for years: sell your crazy somewhere else, . We talked with great ad man Will Robinson about how Dems need a whole new strategy to connect with people. Authentic connection, between neighbors, colleagues, friends and influencers they've know via two-way convos on social media. Democrats need to deliver messages from those whom voters trust (which, speaking as a former ad guy, has been obvious for a long time!). Also, . Much more in here, watch! name: Randy Villegas
subject: Central Valley homes are sinking – and their values are sinking with them.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Villegas for Congress Villegas for Congress
body:
|
# [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112991701/562284104/1981932150?refcode=header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDM2ZWM5MjUtYTM3NC1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=kwucvrmZ4DD_h0d0mv4u1H50Whaqjkbhea8tvl-qWqU=)
A new study just confirmed what we in the Central Valley already feel: **Our homes are sinking – and their values are sinking with them.** | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112991702/562284105/1981932150?refcode=em-8.8.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDM2ZWM5MjUtYTM3NC1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=kwucvrmZ4DD_h0d0mv4u1H50Whaqjkbhea8tvl-qWqU=) **Study links valley's land subsidence to
lower home values**
---
Here’s what’s happening:
When drought hits and surface water runs dry, groundwater gets pumped out faster than it can be replenished. The result? The land literally collapses – and UC Riverside has calculated that single-family homes in the San Joaquin Valley lose between **2.4% and 5.8%** of their value. That’s up to **$16,000 lost per home.**
That’s not just a statistic. That’s real families in our district losing equity. Losing water. Losing peace of mind.
Over-pumping happens when more water is drawn out of underground aquifers than is replaced. Picture a sponge that gets squeezed but never refilled. Over time, it flattens and hardens, and the land above it sinks too.
This is a challenge we can’t afford to ignore. And it’s going to take bold, sustainable solutions and leadership that’s grounded in science and long-term thinking.
That starts with leadership that listens, plans ahead, and puts people before politics.
Thank you,
Professor Randy Villegas

clean_body: | = A new study just confirmed what we in the Central Valley already feel: Our homes are sinking – and their values are sinking with them. | = Study links valley's land subsidence to lower home values Here’s what’s happening: When drought hits and surface water runs dry, groundwater gets pumped out faster than it can be replenished. The result? The land literally collapses – and UC Riverside has calculated that single-family homes in the San Joaquin Valley lose between 2.4% and 5.8% of their value. That’s up to $16,000 lost per home. That’s not just a statistic. That’s real families in our district losing equity. Losing water. Losing peace of mind. Over-pumping happens when more water is drawn out of underground aquifers than is replaced. Picture a sponge that gets squeezed but never refilled. Over time, it flattens and hardens, and the land above it sinks too. This is a challenge we can’t afford to ignore. And it’s going to take bold, sustainable solutions and leadership that’s grounded in science and long-term thinking. That starts with leadership that listens, plans ahead, and puts people before politics. Thank you, Professor Randy Villegas 👋🏻Wait, before you go! = = = | = | With deep ties to our community and a lifelong commitment to justice and equity, Randy is running for Congress in California’s 22nd District to bring bold, compassionate leadership to Washington. To support Randy's campaign = We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Paid for by Villegas for Congress Villegas for Congress PO Box 1346 Visalia, CA 93279 United States

subject: Powered by People just filed a lawsuit against Ken Paxton.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
The gloves are off.
#
The gloves are off.**
**Powered by People just filed a lawsuit against Ken Paxton.** He’s abusing his power to score some political points, but let me tell you right now: We are not going to be intimidated.
This is our moment to fight for our democracy as hard as we possibly can. We’re going to take this fight to Paxton, Abbott, and Trump with everything we have.
_And these thugs are panicking right now._
We aren’t going to pull any punches, but I can tell you right now that this is going to be expensive.
Folks from across the country have generously donated to help us support the brave lawmakers who broke quorum. We will need to raise extra funds to help pay for legal expenses due to their frivolous lawsuit.
[**If you can, please donate any amount that you can to help us fight for our democracy.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20250808-2.8JUPCF&refcode=ema20250808-240298&t=2&akid=240298%2E444211%2EphCM-e)

I’m ready to fight as hard as I possibly can.
Let’s go,
Beto

subject: Democratic Attorneys General are fighting back against Trump

body:

Team —
In a second, we’re going to ask you to help meet our crucial daily fundraising goal, but first, we need to tell you about the brave Attorneys General who are fighting for all of us.
Right now, 23 Democratic Attorneys General are the strongest firewall between Trump and his dreams of total authoritarian control.
In courtrooms across the country, they're fighting back against every unconstitutional executive order, every corrupt power grab, every assault on our democracy.
These 23 AGs have already DEFEATED Trump in court – and they're just getting started:
🔵 Dem AGs stopped Trump from freezing health grants for lifesaving research.
🔵 Dem AGs forced Trump to restore $6.8 billion in school funding he tried to withhold.
🔵 Dem AGs blocked Trump cuts to critical FEMA programs.
Virginia needs an Attorney General who will join those 23 AGs and actually FIGHT to protect us. Let’s be totally clear:
1: Trump’s agenda is hurting Virginia more than other states. Why? Because VA Attorney General Jason Miyares is his loyal foot soldier, who says nothing while Trump fires thousands of federal workers in Virginia – many of them veterans.
2: Trump – who pardoned violent insurrectionists and weaponizes the federal government against his critics – thinks Jason Miyares is doing a "great job" as Virginia's Attorney General.
3: Jay Jones is the only candidate for Attorney General who will join the fight against MAGA extremism instead of rolling over for Trump’s destructive MAGA agenda.
Can you rush $15 or more to help Jay Jones join these 23 Democratic AGs in the fight to stop Trump's authoritarian agenda? We absolutely must hit our $15,000 daily fundraising goal to build the campaign that can defeat Miyares and protect Virginia from Trump's assault on our democracy. » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_new-gen-election-em_q32025?amount=15]

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Victory in Oregon Voter Rolls Lawsuit
body:
Nashville Shooter Manifesto Update

[INSIDE JW]

COURT GREENLIGHTS JUDICIAL WATCH LAWSUIT TO CLEAN UP OREGON VOTER
ROLLS

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=35823&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g/dj1ZZVFjUmdrQWw0ayZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=88dc4c8e3e2ed86ebc7a3fa5f6086299c7b675be4a665c0d541a002ff8e3f2d2]

Judicial Watch continues to make progress in cleaning up voter rolls
across the country.

A federal court in Oregon ruled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=35821&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtb3JlZ29uLW52cmEtZGVjaXNpb24tMDE3ODMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=0faf82ffee65be2a49c7e358fa92494c834c0776327474f8185ae1789940cd18]
that our lawsuit filed on behalf of the Constitution Party of Oregon
and two registered voters to force the clean-up of Oregon’s voter
rolls may proceed.

Our lawsuit alleges that Oregon violated the National Voter
Registration Act
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=435&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3YvY3J0L25hdGlvbmFsLXZvdGVyLXJlZ2lzdHJhdGlvbi1hY3QtMTk5My1udnJhP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=f158da4a5ae1de35e565a6a8f53da89cd8322253d93d918bf210c72ca3be414b]
(NVRA), which requires states to “conduct a general program that
makes a reasonable effort to remove” from the official voter rolls
“the names of ineligible voters” who have died or changed
residence.

In June, the U.S. Department of Justice filed a statement of interest
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=35459&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY29tcGVsLW9yZWdvbi10by1jbGVhbi12b3Rlci1yb2xscy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=79ae1298842fa15c82a816073e49517f8ad55a6d44b8f95068a5fc24a9a886d1]
in
this lawsuit.

In a June 6 Justice Department press release, Assistant Attorney
General for the Civil Rights Division Harmeet Dhillon said
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=35438&F=T&&hash=54825e4283107d4e78c0bc979aba5526a12ac0fb463e7c6d5e147d802b57db72
“Accurate voter registration rolls are critical to ensure that
elections in Oregon are conducted fairly, accurately, and without
fraud.... States have specific obligations under the list maintenance
provisions of the NVRA, and the Department of Justice will vigorously
enforce those requirements.”

We initially filed the lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=33760&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvb3JlZ29uLWNsZWFuLXVwLXZvdGVyLXJvbGxzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=39b27e83ca79bb9fe9d5ce44e69256c7fe13213ae7767178329c679176397cf3]
in
October 2024 to enforce basic voter list maintenance provisions under
Section 8 of the NVRA after uncovering
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=33275&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvb3JlZ29uLXRvLWNsZWFuLXZvdGVyLXJlZ2lzdHJhdGlvbi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=4b43ca6f53ac732e6ac912193dbd2df24c9aaa2819573a121c552a382a40df3e]
a
broad failure to clean up voter rolls in dozens of Oregon counties
(_Judicial Watch, et al. v. The State of Oregon et al._
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=33744&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL29yLW52cmEtY29tcGxhaW50LTAxNzgzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=3bcbb1a7c80c081e8328230be286e16ae73fc909d5cf59db4101e40b2f3923cd]
(No. 6:24-cv-01783)).

In 2018, the Supreme Court confirmed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=26310&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3VwcmVtZWNvdXJ0Lmdvdi9vcGluaW9ucy8xN3BkZi8xNi05ODBfZjJxMy5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=1d1244a9a7e3186f078e968636ba0553fa3d9278af94361dcddcd24ee326f4bb]
that
such removals are mandatory.

In our complaint, we argue that Oregon’s voter rolls contain large
numbers of old, inactive registrations and that 29 of Oregon’s 36
counties removed few or no registrations as required by federal
election law. We assert that Oregon and 35 of its counties had overall
registration rates exceeding 100%; and that Oregon has the highest
known inactive registration rate of any state in the nation.

Recently, federal courts in California and Illinois separately ruled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=35799&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbGF3c3VpdHMtdG8tY2xlYW4tdXAtdm90ZXItcm9sbHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=3ffca2533bc2dd25e07948d2d4a3492d4dca7643bff693d9ece8d8bc8133401b]
that
our lawsuits may proceed against those states to force them to clean
up their voter rolls.

Judicial Watch announced
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=35168&F=T&&hash=54a98dc0af766e1e8cc838fce97a5fdf7566055cdbed1702d666a75e847d5956]
in May that its work led to the removal of more than five million
ineligible names from voter rolls nationwide.

We applaud the court’s decision to allow our case to continue in our
effort to clean up voter rolls in Oregon. We now have three federal
lawsuits against three states to clean potentially millions of names
from the voter rolls.

As you know, we are a national leader in voting integrity and voting
rights. Our team of experienced voting rights attorneys stopped
discriminatory elections in Hawaii and cleaned up voter rolls across
the country, among other achievements
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=35695&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdGFnL2VsZWN0aW9uK2xhdy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=9397a9e3d745abe0c3f21cda7e7ee80355607c49f190c9e5632b403f671aff4b].

Robert Popper, a Judicial Watch senior attorney, leads our election
law program. Popper was previously in the Voting Section of the Civil
Rights Division of the Justice Department, where he managed voting
rights investigations, litigations, consent decrees, and settlements
in dozens of states.

Recently, the Commonwealth of Kentucky reported that 735,000
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=34903&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtYWRhbXMta3ktbnZyYS1jb21wbGlhbmNlLW5vdGljZS0wMDA5NC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=bc0d19050231e9e2e7b38d34efcd9fa4ab74a2999b47431616febf3c8d30105d]
ineligible voter registrations had been removed from its voter rolls
since 2019 by the State Board of Elections as part of its 2018 consent
decree settling a lawsuit by Judicial Watch.

As part of its 2022 settlement
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=75849&L=28646&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctYW5kLW55Yy1zZXR0bGUtbGF3c3VpdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=f85eb002f4b9dfa8334e25686194139c3301a613cd8d8d197cbaf3272fc1aa08],
New York
City alone has removed 918,139 ineligible names from its rolls. Recent
data show 477,056

> grants that fund all sorts of preposterous Diversity, Equity and
> Inclusion (DEI) initiatives at dozens of universities and a few
> nonprofits throughout the United States. The cash is disbursed by
> the National Science Foundation (NSF), established by Congress in
> 1950 to promote the progress of science, advance national health,
> prosperity, and welfare and to secure national defense. Instead,
> under the Biden administration the agency went on a remarkable DEI
> spending frenzy that has supported a multitude of exclusionary
> leftist projects at private and public academic institutions
> nationwide as well as organizations that claim to advance minority
> causes.

> One of the country’s largest public universities alone has lost
> tens of millions of dollars in NSF grants identified by the Trump
> administration as supporting blatant DEI projects that are unlikely
> to further the agency’s mission. With an undergraduate enrollment
> of 142,000, Arizona State University (ASU) has received around $28.5
> million from the NSF for over two dozen DEI initiatives recently
> placed on the chopping block. Among them is a $2.4 million project
> called Black Girls as Creators described as an intersectional
> learning ecosystem toward gendered racial equity in artificial
> intelligence education to help black girls focus on
> intersectionality and racial equity. An “institutional
> transformational” project that aims to reshape faculty policies
> for gender equity and intersectionality received nearly $3 million.
> A program that positioned engineering faculty to support black
> students through awareness, knowledge, capacity building and
> community got $733,633 and over half a million dollars went to a
> community transformation experiment that claims to increase justice,
> equity, diversity, and inclusion in academic standards.

> Public universities on opposite sides of the country are losing a
> total of $2 million for identical projects titled “Challenging
> Anti-Black-Racism in Civil and Environmental Engineering
> Curriculum” that claim to serve the national public interest by
> establishing education practices that acknowledge low-income
> communities of color are disproportionately exposed to air and water
> pollution. The University of South Florida in Tampa received $1.5
> million for the environmental justice initiative and the University
> of California Berkeley $500,000. UC Berkeley also lost a $1.6
> million allocation from the NSF to help center scientific and
> environmental literacy around the voices of communities of color
> since, according to the grant document, the entire field is
> “aligned with dominant views that can marginalize, exclude, and
> erase the knowledge and expertise of communities of color.” The
> renowned northern California university asserts that its study will
> “contribute new understandings of how race and culture influence
> learning, as well as how racism and biases have shaped research to
> date.”

> The list of canceled DEI science grants is extensive with many of
> the hefty awards—most around a million dollars—using similar
> language in the name of combatting widespread discrimination in the
> field. The University of Denver in Colorado got north of a million
> dollars to mobilize equity and raise inclusivity in Science,
> Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) by creating a
> special coalition that will hire more faculty with “intersectional
> identities” and increase women as well as other underrepresented
> groups. The University of Missouri in St. Louis also got over a
> million dollars to advance equity in STEM academic careers by
> cultivating a climate where women, African Americans, Hispanics, and
> other historically underrepresented groups will thrive. The City
> University of New York (CUNY) received $999,839 to implement
> institutional transformation emphasizing the advancement of Black,
> Indigenous, and People of Color (BIPOC) faculty. A south Florida
> public university received $999,853 to focus on inclusive support
> for gender diversity and intersectional minorities, especially
> Latina, and African American women in hiring and retention.

> Among the nonprofits that saw their government money cut is the
> Children’s Museum of Pittsburgh, which received nearly $600,000 to
> address accessibility and inclusion issues at visitor-serving
> organizations such as museums, botanical gardens, and zoos that
> “tend to serve disproportionately white, able-bodied, and
> high-income audiences.” A Public Broadcasting Station (PBS) in New
> York, got over $3 million from the government to better engage
> underserved families in STEM by targeting low-income Latinx (a
> gender-neutral term fabricated by the left) families with media and
> text-based mechanisms. The stated goal is to build knowledge about
> how innovative, culturally responsive tools can help Latinx and
> low-income families engage in fun STEM learning at home. These are
